programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Andy hates girlfriend Alyssa's 'demon cat' Tosh, who turned after he moved in with her. Eric and Kaotar's problem cat, Vincent, tries anything to escape

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: A cat called Godzilla is not as fearsome as she sounds, and is terrified of the other cat in the house

programme.title: Treehouse Masters

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Investigating what dogs see when they watch TV, how snakes eat prey twice the size of their heads, and how cuttlefish are able to vanish without a trace

programme.title: Extinct or Alive
programme.desc: Forrest Galante sets off on a dangerous, shark-infested search for the extinct Caribbean monk seal. He and the crew come face-to-face with a giant tiger shark

programme.title: Edge of Alaska

programme.title: Fish or Die

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Neil breaks through to the Mother Lode mine, but the locals are too busy defending against waking bears to stop him and Jenny goes on a solo bear-hunt of her own

programme.title: The Haunted
programme.desc: Laura chooses to ignore the strange behaviour of her dogs, until she and her daughter see a ghostly man in military uniform in their home

programme.title: Mosquito
programme.desc: Leading scientists share controversial means of dealing with mosquitos, which pose a unique threat as the single greatest agent of death in modern human history

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.sub-title: Space Bugs and Squirrel Mind Maps
programme.desc: A squirrel's ability to hide up to 10,000 nuts in one season - and still find them again. Plus, alligators' fondness for music and whether a bug can survive in space

programme.title: Crikey! It's the Irwins

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: The wardens have to relocate a 500lb alligator roaming a suburban area, then uncover a large marijuana hoard hidden in a wildlife preserve

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff heads to Texas to help out rescued animals after Hurricane Harvey. Back in Denver, a woman brings in Bear, who needs surgery after eating a sock

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Rocky, a young pitbull, fights for his life after being bitten by a rattlesnake, while Sassquatch the cat suffers after a fire at his owner's apartment

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees
programme.desc: Tia gives a hybrid wolfdog a second chance. At the vet, Lizzy is forced to make a tough call for a new rescue and later helps a pair of hearing-impaired canines

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy comes to the aid of George and Beth, who are virtual prisoners in their own home due to their vicious kitty Reese

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Alan and Shannon wanted a cat to cuddle, but instead they got Barnabas - a hissing, flesh-ripping feline who can only be calmed by opera music

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson makes a treehouse for American football player Antonio Brown, who wants a window wall that overlooks a basketball court and football field

programme.title: Meet the Orangutans
programme.desc: Chiquita was only two months old when she arrived at Sepilok sanctuary. Now aged three, her surrogate attachment to one of the staff is causing developmental issues

programme.title: Meet the Orangutans
programme.desc: There is a baby boom at the sanctuary as new arrivals mix with news of more on the way. The dominant male known as CID is thought to be the father of all offspring - but may not be

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: The birds that can fly as high as a passenger jet, and why tiny jumping spiders are dancing for their lives. Plus, how West Coast squirrels have outsmarted rattlesnakes

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.sub-title: The Secret to a Long Life
programme.desc: How homing pigeons are able to find their way home, how spiders lure bugs into their trap, and how some fish can survive for years above ground

programme.title: Little Giants
programme.desc: In the arid scrubland of Tinaja, New Mexico, Bradley and Billy track down the giant scolopendra centipede. Armed with venomous claws, the animal is a relentless killer

programme.title: Little Giants
programme.desc: Bradley and Billy are in Great Karoo, South Africa, searching for a majestic micro-dragon - the armadillo girdled lizard. It has a formidable defence mechanism

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: The hidden city inside the mountain is within reach, but exploration means convincing locals for help, and the trailblazing ranchers are in danger

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Long Island medium Theresa Caputo asks Wayde and Brett to build her a mermaid tank and a koi pond

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Host and actor Mario Lopez helps the ATM team transform a 1990s coin-operated video-game console into an interactive aquarium that features an exercise bike

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Counters moved to a remote plot in Colorado for much-needed tranquillity. But two years in, their crops are failing and their teenage kids are bored

programme.title: Great White Abyss
programme.desc: A team of experts travels to great white hotspot Guadalupe Island in search of the biggest sharks of their kind, using deep-water cage and submarine dives

programme.title: The Haunted
programme.desc: The paranormal team investigates when a girl is attacked by an invisible entity

programme.title: Lair of the Killer Crocs
programme.desc: The number of crocodiles in Australia has boomed - and they are getting bigger and more deadly. This documentary investigates attempts to relocate them

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: How does a fish make a weapon out of water? Why do turtles always return to the same beach to lay their eggs? Why do rhinos all poop in the same place?

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: The process that caterpillars go through to transform into magnificent butterflies while in their cocoon. Plus, the facts about cats' tongues

programme.title: Meet the Superbrains
programme.desc: The series celebrating pioneering research scientists concludes with a look at the work being done by engineers seeking to revolutionise global transport

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy, after some initial complications, is asked to be best man at his friend Frank's wedding. He ends up in trouble as a result of trying to encourage the idea of marriage in spite of his parents

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs hosts a 1977 edition of the old-time music hall programme, with Larry Grayson, Fenella Fielding, Hinge and Bracket, Anna Sharkey and members of the Players' Theatre, London, entertaining an audience from the stage of the Leeds City Varieties

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jayne Middlemiss presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on November 11, 1999. Featuring Tin Tin Out feat Emma Bunton, Savage Garden, Beck, Another Level, Jennifer Lopez, Mr Vegas, Children's Promise and Geri Halliwell

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Peter Powell present an edition first aired on May 22, 1986. Featuring performances by Jaki Graham, Billy Ocean, Simply Red, Spitting Image, the B-52s, Patti LaBelle and Michael McDonald, and the Matchroom Mob and Chas & Dave

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on May 23, 1991. Featuring T'Pau, Simple Minds and Deacon Blue

programme.title: Sings Dylan II
programme.desc: Cover versions of Bob Dylan songs from the 1960s to the present, sourced from the BBC archive. Featuring classic tracks old and new, plus a few surprises. Performers include Joan Baez, Siouxsie and the Banshees, the Hollies, Adele, Julie Felix, Richie Havens, Bryan Ferry and KT Tunstall

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs hosts a 1977 edition of the old-time music hall programme, with Larry Grayson, Fenella Fielding, Hinge and Bracket, Anna Sharkey and members of the Players' Theatre, London, entertaining an audience from the stage of the Leeds City Varieties

programme.title: Dusty
programme.desc: Entertainment showcase with the singer and her backing band of Madeline Bell, Lesley Duncan and Barbara Moore, with a guest appearance by Spanish ventriloquist Señor Wences. First shown in September 1966

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: DCI Adams' investigations into a spate of domestic robberies result in an accusation of sexual harassment against him from a nurse. Hetty is convinced he has been wrongly accused and investigates the claim from her hospital bed

programme.title: My Favourite Cake (2024)
programme.desc: Mahin, a 70-year-old widow living alone in Tehran, decides to break her solitary routine and revitalise her love life, but an unexpected encounter quickly evolves into an unforgettable evening. Iranian romantic comedy drama, starring Lili Farhadpour and Esmaeel Mehrabi. In Persian

programme.title: King Otto and Football's Greek Gods
programme.desc: The story of how Otto Rehhagel coached Greece to become the unlikely winners of Euro 2004 and how two contrasting cultures came together to write a new chapter of Greek mythology. After he accomplished every major success in Germany, Rehhagel made the decision to work in a foreign country with the underachieving Greek national team

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth discovers Emmet has planned a piano concert for the senior citizens at the church hall, and decides to treat the lucky audience to her own brand of sing-song. Suburban comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and David Griffin

programme.title: The Classical Collection
programme.desc: The natural world has always been a powerful inspiration to composers. From vast forests and tiny fish to wild storms and epic seascapes, this programme takes viewers on an evocative journey through some of the best-loved musical responses to the living planet. Including works by Vivaldi, Reiner, Vaughan Williams and Debussy

programme.title: Dusty
programme.desc: Entertainment showcase with the singer and her backing band of Madeline Bell, Lesley Duncan and Barbara Moore, with a guest appearance by Spanish ventriloquist Señor Wences. First shown in September 1966

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: DCI Adams' investigations into a spate of domestic robberies result in an accusation of sexual harassment against him from a nurse. Hetty is convinced he has been wrongly accused and investigates the claim from her hospital bed

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the history of the fishing village of Port Isaac in Cornwall, discovering a place where people exploited a range of natural resources to make a living and find profits far beyond Britain's shores

programme.title: Afoot Again in the Past
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark visits the 13th-century Inchmahome Priory, which was Mary, Queen of Scots' former residence and is situated on an island in the Lake of Menteith, Perth & Kinross. First broadcast in 2002

programme.title: Crooks Anonymous (1962)
programme.desc: A supposedly reformed petty thief is tempted back into a life of crime by the thought of one last big heist. Comedy, starring Leslie Phillips, Stanley Baxter, Dick Emery, Wilfrid Hyde White and Julie Christie, making her feature-film debut

programme.title: Being Stanley Baxter
programme.desc: A profile of the Glasgow-born actor, comedian and impressionist, who died in December 2025 at the age of 99, charting his extraordinary professional journey and personal life by drawing on rare archive footage and candid interviews. The film reflects the complex aspects of his long and distinguished life as well as celebrating his comedic genius, particularly his impressionist and satirical work

programme.title: The Magic of Dance with Margot Fonteyn
programme.desc: The former ballerina explores the story of the romantic ballet and its most prominent advocates, tracing the story of Marie Taglioni, one of the first ballerinas to dance on the tips of her toes and known to have epitomised the era

programme.title: Les Sylphides
programme.desc: A studio performance of the ballet by Michel Fokine, first broadcast on April 3, 1953, and starring Alicia Markova, John Field, Violetta Elvin and Svetlana Beriosova

programme.title: Omnibus
programme.desc: Theatre and film director Lindsay Anderson talsk to ballet superstar Rudolf Nureyev. First broadcast in 1974

programme.title: King Otto and Football's Greek Gods
programme.desc: The story of how Otto Rehhagel coached Greece to become the unlikely winners of Euro 2004 and how two contrasting cultures came together to write a new chapter of Greek mythology. After he accomplished every major success in Germany, Rehhagel made the decision to work in a foreign country with the underachieving Greek national team

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms explores the handsome heroes and pin-ups of the silver screen, including Paul Newman, Robert Redford, Jane Russell and Marilyn Monroe. Plus, hearing from some of the stars themselves talking about what their sex symbol status meant to them

programme.title: Eve and Marilyn
programme.desc: Eve Arnold, one of the world's leading photo-journalists, remembers photographing Marilyn Monroe over the ten crucial years of her stardom and decline. Going back to the original contact sheets and colour material, Arnold tells of their friendship and what it was like as a woman to photograph Marilyn Monroe. First broadcast September 8, 1987

programme.title: Scene by Scene
programme.desc: A rare interview with former Hollywood sex symbol Jane Russell. She talks about her difficult childhood as the eldest and only girl of six siblings, her film career and working with Marilyn Monroe

programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Natalie Cassidy joins Matt Tebbutt and chefs Tom Parker Bowles and Tony Singh in an episode for BBC Caring Matters Week. Helen McGinn picks the drinks

programme.title: Anna Haugh's Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: The chef takes a culinary tour of Ireland, beginning by travelling to the city of Cork with comedian Katherine Ryan. They pay a visit to the Ballymaloe Cookery School and one of the oldest food markets in Europe in the hope of banishing Katherine's memories of her grandmother's cooking

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Mould - Always Believe in Your Soul
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a Hampshire house with a mould problem, Martel Maxwell needs to watch her step in Country Durham and Dion Dublin finds an overgrown garden in Warrington

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the show from Nottinghamshire, and meets a former contestant with a world record collection of bagpipes. With experts Ben Cooper and Izzie Balmer

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Briony May Williams is in Herefordshire, helping a couple who want to buy their first home together so they can finally get their collection of potted plants in the ground. Briony also meets another couple who developed a natural-cider business after they made their dream country escape, in search of a more self-sufficient lifestyle

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph repurposes an old ladder found at the tip and challenges designer Ayo to unlock the potential of a £2 table with a bold design

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Helen and Kevin's Derbyshire garden is dominated by three things - a patio that looks too big for the space, a breeze-block wall that looks ugly and a shed that sticks out like a sore thumb. As they near retirement, the couple want to transform the space into a relaxing low-maintenance seaside garden inspired by Portugal. Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill take on the challenge

programme.title: Shrek (2001)
programme.desc: Anti-social ogre Shrek resolves to stop an evil ruler from banishing fairy-tale characters to his swampland home. However, the reluctant hero ends up forced to rescue the beautiful Princess Fiona from a castle guarded by a dragon, aided only by a wisecracking donkey. Animated comedy, with the voices of Mike Myers, John Lithgow, Cameron Diaz, Eddie Murphy and Vincent Cassel

programme.title: Celebrity Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the high stakes celebrity quiz. Rugby player Joe Marler, actor Jennie McAlpine, comedian Geoff Norcott and presenter Jean Johannson must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for the charity of their choice with each correct answer

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the comedy quiz show in which a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win some brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he is joined by Jonathan Ross, Joanna Page, Harry Redknapp, Joe Swash, Susan Wokoma and Shazia Mirza

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: The comedian hosts an evening of entertainment, giving Alison Hammond a rude awakening to play the Midnight Game Show, presenting Kaiser Chiefs frontman Ricky Wilson with the chance to become the Unexpected Star Star of the Show and coming up with a witty text message on singer Anne-Marie's mobile to send to all her contacts. There's also a performance by James Blunt

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Stevie discovers Matty covered for her mistake. As her confidence is given a further blow by a troubling letter, she fears that revealing the truth will bring other secrets to light. Faith is hopeful Pearl can be discharged soon, but Rash cautions she still needs to learn how to feed properly. Nicole stands to lose everything, and Teddy lies to Jacob

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: Drama, starring Jessica Raine. Zoe and her family arrive on a remote Greek island, hoping a holiday reunion with friends will be an escape from reality. But her husband Dan's dark secret threatens to ruin everything, while a hedonistic first night party sets off a chain of events that will change them all forever. Zoe begins to wonder if there are bigger forces at play

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: On an ill-fated group boat trip, Zoe tries to pretend last night's betrayal didn't mean anything. But Dan's behaviour threatens to push her over the edge. Nat uses the kiss to drive a wedge between Jacob and his boyfriend Will, who has shown up uninvited. Solomon is the only one who doesn't know what happened last night - does Zoe tell him and destroy two marriages? Jess is forced to take drastic action to avoid confrontation

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Zara Larsson: Radio 1's Big Weekend Sunderland
programme.desc: The Swedish singer gives her fourth performance at the festival, following the release of her latest album Midnight Sun. Still in her teens when she made her first appearance at Big Weekend in 2017, this year's festival from Herrington Country Park marks her first time topping the bill

programme.title: Saturday Highlights: Radio 1's Big Weekend Sunderland
programme.desc: Highlights from Saturday's performances on a day that has seen the likes of Zara Larsson, Ellie Goulding, Skye Newman, Louis Tomlinson, James Blake and Florence Road take to the Main Stage and New Music Stage in Sunderland's Herrington Country Park

programme.title: The Football Interview
programme.sub-title: Football Eras: Andy Robertson meets Kenny Dalglish
programme.desc: Liverpool legend Dalgish talks to his Scottish compatriot about his time at the Reds and his hopes for the FIFA World Cup

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the comedy quiz show in which a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win some brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he is joined by Jonathan Ross, Joanna Page, Harry Redknapp, Joe Swash, Susan Wokoma and Shazia Mirza

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg
programme.desc: The big names behind the big stories. Laura Kuenssberg talks to those making the news, inside and outside politics.

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy makers on the key issues

programme.title: Pentecost
programme.desc: A celebration of Pentecost from Hope Street Church in Wrexham. Members of the congregation share inspirational stories of finding their faith, alongside reflections of how this new and growing church is playing a part in local community regeneration. The service is led by Andy and Rachel Kitchen, with music from Hope Street's worship band

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The team follows a young couple as they transform their first home in Haydock, Merseyside, and a developer in Blackpool as he restores a fire-damaged house for the rental market. Plus, a family creates a four-bedroom house from a small cottage in a Norfolk village

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Alex Scott and guests preview the final round of Premier League matches, and look back at the show's best moments, as the programme comes to an end after 52 years

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones is in Bristol to explore the spiritual inspiration of John and Charles Wesley, and to introduce classic Wesley hymns from the oldest Methodist building in the world

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Ben Fogle presents an appeal on behalf of Hearing Dogs for Deaf People, a charity that provide support and trained assistance dogs to those living with deafness or hearing loss

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Perth and Kinross, helping a young couple with a budget of £350,000 to find an ideal country home for them and their two young sons

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: Wildlife in America's western states, from coyotes in Yellowstone using geothermal heat to survive the winter to bison on the Great Plains facing fierce rivals to win mates as fires renew the grasslands. In Colorado, hundreds of rattlesnakes emerge after winter, while on Nebraska's Platte River, half a million sandhill cranes converge for their courtship dances. Narrated by Tom Hanks

programme.title: Minions: The Rise of Gru (2022)
programme.desc: Young Gru attends an interview to join a diabolical dream team christened the Vicious 6, but Belle Bottom, Jean-Clawed, Nun-chuck, Svengeance and Stronghold cruelly dismiss the boy. In retaliation for the ageist snub, the resourceful tyke steals an ancient amulet that can harness the power of the 12 creatures of the Chinese zodiac. Animated comedy prequel, featuring the voices of Steve Carell, Alan Arkin, Taraji P Henson and Jean-Claude Van Damme

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents the results and this afternoon's news from the final round of Premier League fixtures

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show 2026
programme.desc: Sophie Raworth and Adam Frost look back on the highlights of the week at the RHS Chelsea Flower Show. They showcase the must-have plants, the award-winning gardens and latest design trends. They also remember some of the famous faces who have visited the horticultural show this year

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from Firstsite contemporary art gallery in Colchester, , where finds include a creepy goblin figurine, a 17th-century amulet, a porcelain plaque discovered buried in a box bought at auction, and a Hans Coper vase, originally gifted by the celebrated ceramist himself. Fiona is joined by Lee Young to learn about state-of-the-art techniques in ceramic repairs

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: A food shack in a fishing village is burned down with its owner inside. John takes a holiday in the village to recover from recent troubles, but cannot resist a mystery. Janie struggles to balance her new responsibilities with the detective she wants to be as they uncover the connections between fierce business rivalries, a waning fishing industry and a rare breeding seal colony

programme.title: Dear England
programme.desc: Fact-based drama, starring Joseph Fiennes as Gareth Southgate, exploring the England manager's innovative methods to turn around the team's fortunes. Southgate takes on a team at an all-time low and a jaded fanbase after an embarrassing defeat to Iceland. Haunted by his own disastrous penalty miss as a player, he enlists the aid of a psychologist ( Jodie Whittaker) to change the team's mindset and tackle their biggest fear

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Kelly Cates is joined by Alan Shearer and Wayne Rooney for action from the final Premier League matches of the season, including Crystal Palace v Arsenal at Selhurst Park and Manchester City v Aston Villa at Etihad Stadium. The title race between Arsenal and City had been expected to go to the wire, with the Gunners looking to win the league for the first time since 2004, and the Citizens looking to claim the Premier for the seventh time under Pep Guardiola. Plus, Tottenham Hotspur v Everton, West Ham United v Leeds United, Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester United, Burnley v Wolverhampton Wanderers, Fulham v Newcastle United, Liverpool v Brentford, Nottingham Forest v Bournemouth and Sunderland v Chelsea

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Mam manages a team for a local charity football match, where Dickie and Lucinda compete to become the best wags

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet responds to being mugged by becoming a police community support officer. Wesley returns to the area intent on seeking advice from Gaz on the best way to win Donna's heart, while Louise runs into problems as she tries to find a father figure for her daughter. Comedy, starring Sheridan Smith, Will Mellor, Thomas Nelstrop and Kathryn Drysdale

programme.title: The Big Proud Party Agency
programme.desc: Best friends Lauren and Tamara are keen to celebrate their friend Miya, who underwent gender confirmation surgery three years ago

programme.title: The Big Proud Party Agency
programme.desc: Best friends Ryan and Daniel want to throw the mother of all comeback parties for their friend and fellow wrestler Shannon. She badly injured herself almost two years ago performing her signature move and has not stepped into a ring since. Now she has recovered and her friends are keen to celebrate her comeback. Ryan pitches a 1980s arcade-inspired wrestling smackdown, while Teddy campaigns for a Rebellion-themed street party and Christopher wants to create the ultimate fight club in an underground car park. Who will win the business?

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Brothers, flatmates and polar opposites Samir and Nikhil find themselves on an adventure when they accidentally steal a package from their maniac neighbour. One-off comedy short, starring Karan Gill, Ikky Kabir, Eshaan Akbar, Elina Saleh, Ed Kear and Andrea Valls

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip must come to terms with the repercussions of solving the case. However something doesn't seem quite right, and she places herself in danger once more to find out the whole truth. Detective drama, starring Emma Myers

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: There's no place like home when Dickie takes his workmates on a Wizard of Oz night out

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Mam manages a team for a local charity football match, where Dickie and Lucinda compete to become the best wags

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor is given a task he cannot refuse - breaking into the most secure bank in the universe. The Joined by a shape-shifter, a cyber-augmented gamer and, of course, trusty sidekick Clara. they must get past the bank's toughest security feature - a creature that can detect guilt in people's minds. Sci-fi drama, starring Peter Capaldi and Jenna Coleman, with a guest appearance by Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Clara finds the two sides of her life colliding explosively when the Doctor goes undercover as a caretaker at her school. A deadly robot is on the loose, but more worrying for Clara, she has not shared her secret adventures in time and space with Danny, while he very quickly meets with the Doctor's disapproval

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: The joke-shop owner lacks motivation following Gary's departure for Hong Kong, but eventually decides to move on and sets about getting fit and losing weight. However, she first has to negotiate lunch with the girls, and a sushi restaurant spells trouble. Comedy, starring Miranda Hart

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Tilly and Penny organise a charity wine-tasting event, prompting Miranda to do something for a good cause. However, a stint volunteering at a rest home and an attempted parachute jump do not go according to plan. She is overjoyed when old friends Chris and Alison ask her to be godmother to their baby, but she soon remembers what an annoying couple they are, and sets about trying to prove she is not a worthwhile candidate for the role

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Bono is being bullied by another boy at school so his grandmother steps in to sort things out in her inimitable style. When her efforts to help only make things even worse for the lad, a mediation session is called for. Cathy's new French boyfriend doesn't make a great first impression, which could be a problem if he is to survive meeting Mrs Brown, and Grandad is back from hospital with a new stairlift - and a prescription for marijuana. Comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Father Damien organises a neighbourhood patrol to tackle the rising amount of crime in Finglas, but it quickly becomes clear that Agnes and Winnie are not exactly cut out for catching criminals. Meanwhile, Cathy has had yet another disastrous internet date, so Mrs Brown takes some unorthodox steps to sort out her daughter's love life once and for all. Second of two festive specials, starring Brendan O'Carroll, Eilish O'Carroll and Jennifer Gibney

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: A couple of visiting girls catch Conor and Jock's attention, so they decide it's high time they entered the Cork dating scene. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Whalley

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Mairéad and the lads take the family car on a road trip to pick up her unpredictable mother, but disaster strikes. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy, Chris Walley and Hilary Rose

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Sun and sand are the new dream, but the lads cannot afford it, until Billy Murphy turns up with a daring heist idea. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy, Chris Walley and Shane Casey

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: There's no place like home when Dickie takes his workmates on a Wizard of Oz night out

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy prepares for his first gym appearance since announcing his comeback, but with fighter Darren Till throwing threats and chaos into the mix, trainer John pulls Tommy out of the fight claiming he is unwilling to risk his son's future career. It's a crushing blow for Tommy, who's desperate for momentum and purpose. Amid the pressure, there's a flicker of hope - Tommy and partner Molly-Mae Hague are spotted together on New Year's Eve, with a kiss that hints at a brighter future

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy's fight for redemption continues, but this time, it's a battle for his own mind. Struggling to secure a new opponent, he knows a comeback fight is crucial for his mental health. Meanwhile, Molly-Mae's new series trailer drops, pushing Tommy to face his own truths in the toughest interview of his life, where he opens up about his battle with alcohol addiction. Back in training, hope returns as a potential fight looms, leading him and his father John to a career-defining meeting

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet hopes to earn Gaz's respect by making her first arrest as a police community support officer, but finds herself out of her depth. Donna has difficulty coming to terms with her divorce, until she realises the effort Wesley is prepared to make for her. Meanwhile, Louise tries to improve her baby's IQ

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Donna and Gaz's impending divorce prompts them to reminisce about their relationship through a series of fantasies and flashbacks, remembering how they met as children and the first time they slept together. As their nostalgia grows, they try to imagine how life might be without one another, leading them to wonder whether they have made the right decision

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor is given a task he cannot refuse - breaking into the most secure bank in the universe. The Joined by a shape-shifter, a cyber-augmented gamer and, of course, trusty sidekick Clara. they must get past the bank's toughest security feature - a creature that can detect guilt in people's minds. Sci-fi drama, starring Peter Capaldi and Jenna Coleman, with a guest appearance by Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Clara finds the two sides of her life colliding explosively when the Doctor goes undercover as a caretaker at her school. A deadly robot is on the loose, but more worrying for Clara, she has not shared her secret adventures in time and space with Danny, while he very quickly meets with the Doctor's disapproval

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Lauren makes a risky decision. Jasmine, Oscar and Josh spend the day together. Gina fears for her dad

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Vicki and Grant stage an intervention. Mark grows desperate. Josh makes Oscar jealous

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The people of Walford gather for Nigel's funeral. Grant worries about Mark. The Mitchells head off on a mission

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant drowns his sorrows. Nigel leaves Julie a final gift. Lauren puts her family first

programme.title: Jesy Nelson: Odd One Out
programme.desc: The former Little Mix star goes on a journey of rehabilitation, as she opens up about abuse she has suffered at the hands of cyberbullies and its effects on her mental health

programme.title: The Krays (1990)
programme.desc: Biopic of the infamous twin brothers who built up a criminal empire in the East End of London during the 1960s. Seizing territory from an already established rival by force, they quickly rose to prominence in the city's gangland culture. However, over time the brothers began to clash, while enemies stood ready to bring them down. Gangster biopic, starring Gary and Martin Kemp, Billie Whitelaw and Tom Bell.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Documentary following boxer Tommy Fury's return to the ring. Tommy is at his lowest point personally and professionally, living alone in a house that was meant to be his and partner Molly-Mae's 'forever home'. His role as a father to baby Bambi brings both joy and pressure as he tries to convince Molly-Mae he is a changed man. After finally getting the medical all-clear to fight again, Tommy and his trainer and father John prepare for his return to the ring against MMA fighter Darren Till. Team Fury travels to the press conference - with John promising to be on his best behaviour

programme.title: Hunting the Online Sex Predators
programme.desc: James Blake investigates whether social media is promoting harmful content, joining Cheshire Police's consent campaign against online risk and talking to Dr Kaitlyn Regehr, who explains how her research suggests one social media algorithm can quadruple misogynistic content in a week. He learns how it's easier to get a clip removed for copyrighted music than it is to get child abuse content taken down and realises the full extent of reports of harmful content on social media with the help of NCA officers

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy is hopeful that he could be fighting again soon, and he and Molly-Mae are spending more time together. Then, disaster strikes when KSI cancels the night when Roman was due to be in the fighting, claiming illness. John is furious, but decides the boys need cheering up so tells them he is taking them on a camping trip. However, neither Tommy nor Roman share their father's love of the outdoors

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy heads to Budapest to close the deal for the fight, but there's a hitch. With just four weeks to go, John calls the deal off because the money hasn't reached Tommy's account

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: It's Tommy's birthday, but there's no time for cake. With just days to go before his long-awaited comeback fight in Budapest, Tommy is pulled in every direction - juggling press conferences, promo shoots and the pressure of proving he's still got what it takes in the ring

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: After the success of his comeback fight, Tommy's road to redemption hits a major setback - his hand is injured again and needs eight weeks' total rest before doctors will know if he can fight again. The last time he couldn't train, his life spiralled out of control, so now Tommy throws himself into a frenzy of activity - a TV appearance on The Wheel, family days out with Bambi and a final push to finish the home he hopes Molly and Bambi will soon return to

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy's recovery is back on track after he and John put his hand to the test. The pair are training again, and the search for Tommy's next opponent begins. Eager to make the most of family time, Tommy splashes out on a £90,000 motor home for family trips with Molly and Bambi and takes his mother out for lunch and a long-overdue catch-up. Tommy reveals that John has had a recent health scare, so to cheer him up, father and son take their very different caravans to a local steam fair

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: The drama moves to 2008, and after a breakdown, Niall learns that Ruben has returned. Obsession takes hold, and a charged confrontation forces the brothers to face their fractured past. Explosive drama, written by and starring Richard Gadd, with Jamie Bell

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Anna Haugh and Grace Dent present the last quarter-final. The hopefuls begin with an invention test in which they have to serve a sweet or savoury plate of food that makes the most of fruit. Then, restaurant critic Leyla Kazim sets them a brief, asking for a dish that uses an ingredient they used to hate when they were children

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The team travel to the Royal Navy Air Station in Yeovilton, Somerset, where they are recruited to transform the communal spaces - officially known as mess decks - used by the new recruits for socialising, eating and relaxing. The six designers are given a room each and Michelle Ogundehin's brief is to create spaces that are practical and comfortable, to provide an escape from the regimented daily routine of life on the air station - and it isn't long before the beige walls and boring tables are being replaced by something much more colourful. What will Michelle and guest judge Kelly Holmes - who spent 10 years in the military herself - make of the changes?

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: The week reaches its finale as Vernon Kay hosts the last stop on this week's UK-wide adventure. Anneka Rice, Shane Todd and Remi Burgz have one final shot at glory, with Paul Gorton leading an unreliable tour of Coleraine

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: After Pancake saves him from a mishap, Flutter vows to repay the debt, but his risk-averse behaviour drives Pan to distraction until a real emergency provides an opportunity

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Cosima needs to reassure Big Lizard that he is definitely her best friend for ever

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasy and the chipmunk fight over a toy, which leads to bad consequences. When the ducklings realise their father has caused damage to the farm, they decide to improvise a trial

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes' quest on Spookyween is to stop whatever is menacing the pumpkin patch, finding out that a headless skeleton is using pumpkins as a head

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: It seems an epic battle took place in the cabin, and Grizzy is missing

programme.title: Duck & Frog
programme.desc: Duck is at the museum to give a really boring talk about dinosaurs, but Frog steps in and somehow manages to hatch a baby dinosaur from an ancient egg

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons
programme.desc: Liko and Roy reunite with one of their friends from the Rising Volt Tacklers

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Adam and Joe finally find out which brother is funnier as they take to the stage and perform their best comedy acts to a huge crowd of kids

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Dynamite ruins a celebrity masterpiece when Nitro and Comet team up to turn the art world upside down in a hilarious paint-splattered prank

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.sub-title: Matilda the Musical, and The Mandalorian and Grogu
programme.desc: The RSC production of Matilda the Musical performs When I Grow Up, and Hacker meets the stars of new film The Mandalorian and Grogu! And Abby gets a starring role in the new series of Odd Squad.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Current affairs reports aimed at a younger audience

programme.title: GI Blues (1960)
programme.desc: A guitar-strumming soldier bets he can win the hardened heart of an unresponsive West German cabaret dancer. She rejects him when she learns about the wager, but by then he has already genuinely fallen in love with her. Musical, starring Elvis Presley, Juliet Prowse, James Douglas and Robert Ivers. Songs include Blue Suede Shoes, Tonight Is So Right For Love and the title number

programme.title: Funny Face (1957)
programme.desc: A high-flying photographer arranges a photoshoot in a Greenwich bookshop, where his head is turned by one of the employees. He persuades the shy bookworm to model for him in Paris, and romance blossoms between them as he endeavours to transform her into a fashion icon. Musical, starring Fred Astaire, Audrey Hepburn, Kay Thompson, Michel Auclair and Robert Flemyng, with songs by George and Ira Gershwin

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Charlton Athletic v Leicester City (Kick-off 12.30pm). Coverage of the relegation play-off, held at The Valley. Charlton looked set for automatic promotion to WSL1 until they were overtaken by Birmingham City and Crystal Palace in the final round of fixtures after a 2-0 defeat at the hands of the former. Leicester endured a miserable top-flight campaign, finishing bottom of the table, with only the expansion of the league preventing them from going down automatically

programme.title: Great Bear Stakeout
programme.desc: The second of two programmes about the grizzlies of Alaska focuses on the bears during the world-famous salmon run, during which they must catch hundreds of fish if they are to bulk up to survive the harsh winter months. Parsnip and her cub Pushki have returned to the bay to feed on the salmon - but with predatory adults all around and fierce competition for the food, the mother must protect her baby as they prepare to hibernate. Narrated by Billy Connolly

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Caroline Hawley appraise items at the National Maritime Museum in Falmouth, Cornwall, turning up a few gems including a model yacht and a Newlyn copper tray. Presenter Paul Martin investigates the work of controversial local artist Henry Scott Tuke

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Barcelona v Lyon (Kick-off 5.00pm). All the action from the showpiece match at Ullevaal Stadion in Oslo, Norway. Barca are looking to regain the trophy they won three times between 2021 and 2024 after finishing as runners-up to Arsenal last season, and have been in fine form in the knockout rounds, thrashing neighbours Real Madrid 12-2 on aggregate, before overcoming Bayern Munich 5-3 over two legs in the last four. However, they face a Lyon side who have shown great resolve so far, overcoming first-leg deficits against both Wolfsburg and the holders to reach this stage

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton's loch-hopping journey begins on the west coast and ends inland at Loch Ness. Along the way he hears about Scotland's last outlaw, meets a couple who had a memorable encounter with a monster from the deep called Lizzie, visits a ruined bridge where the first shots were fired of the '45 and meets a man who, when still a schoolboy, met the fastest man on water

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show 2026
programme.desc: Monty Don and Rachel de Thame look back at the highlights of their week, celebrating some of this year's inspirational garden designs and the horticultural excellence on display in the Great Pavilion. Plus, they'share the latest planting ideas and design trends that have been revealed this year

programme.title: Cher at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage of the singer's performances on BBC programmes, including Believe, Love and Understanding, and Walking in Memphis, as well as her first UK chart-topper I Got You Babe, recorded with ex-husband and singing partner Sonny Bono. Other highlights include her 1971 hit Gypsys, Tramps and Thieves, her cover of the Walker Brothers' The Sun Ain't Gonna Shine Anymore, and the 1995 Comic Relief single Love Can Build a Bridge, which Cher recorded with Chrissie Hynde, Neneh Cherry and Eric Clapton

programme.title: Cher Meets Rylan
programme.desc: The singer and actress chats to the Radio 2 presenter about her album Christmas, her biggest hits and her incredible life and career. Throughout the show there is archive footage from across the decades, illustrating Cher's glittering music career, her success in film, the many memorable fashion choices, and how she has influenced so many

programme.title: My Week with Marilyn (2011)
programme.desc: A film student gets a job as a production assistant on the set of movie The Prince and the Showgirl. He bears witness to the clash of conflicting personalities between leads Marilyn Monroe and Laurence Olivier, and becomes a confidant of the troubled, lonely Hollywood actress. Fact-based drama, starring Michelle Williams, Eddie Redmayne, Kenneth Branagh, Judi Dench and Emma Watson

programme.title: Radio 1's Big Weekend
programme.desc: Performances from the final day of the event at Herrington Country Park, featuring all the action from the Main Stage and the New Music Stage, with Olivia Dean, CMAT, Niall Horan, Myles Smith, Ezra Collective and Holly Humberstone among the acts scheduled to appear

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: A community service group comes under suspicion when one of their members is pushed off the tower of a ruined castle around which the group are working. But John's relationship with Janie's mother disrupts their ability to crack the case. Comedy crime drams, starring Timothy Spall and Gwyneth Keyworth, with Jane Horrocks and Alexandra Roach

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at themes beginning with the letter E, as part of her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden takes to the skies above Sussex in a hot-air balloon. She drops in at the gardens of West Dean, where she witnesses the fruits of a 20-year labour of love, and marvels at the transformation of a former car park into an Italian landscape at Arundel Castle. Plus, the contribution the Romans made to British gardening

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Joe Crowley explore the River Leven in Fife, revealing how this industrial waterway shaped communities for centuries and how it is being transformed. Anita joins archaeologists on a major new excavation digging into the river's hidden past, before meeting the people helping reconnect local communities with the water for the first time in generations. At Loch Leven, Joe gets hands-on with one of the river's last working sluices to discover how its waters powered industry, and why it still plays an important role today. Meanwhile, on his farm in the Cotswolds, Adam Henson's annual spring plant surveys reveal how the landscape is changing, year by year

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: As spring turns to summer, Dougie Vipond is in the Cairngorms National Park to find out how the authorities are protecting precious habitats from the risk of wildfire, while Cammy Wilson is on his Ayrshire farm at the peak of his lambing season. Anne Lundon ventures to the Isle of May to find out how extreme weather is driving apart one species of life pairing birds and and pays a visit to the lighthouse where her grandfather was posted. Journalist Rachel Bell follows in the footsteps of the Highlander who left her stylish mark in Braemar

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson and Brian Cunningham focus on plants that attract bees, as well as discussing the best summer bulbs to plant now for fragrance and colour. Brian is back in the vegetable plot to continue his summer-long guide to productive gardening for beginners and there is also a demonstration of how to prune spring-flowering shrubs

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents more highlights, with recipes from Vivek Singh, Emily English and Paul A Young, while broadcaster and actor Ore Oduba faces his food heaven or hell

programme.title: Andi Oliver's Fabulous Feasts
programme.desc: Andi meets Anna and Rebecca, artists who run a community project in a deprived neighbourhood of Stoke-of-Trent. The chef wants to shine a light on their achievements by throwing an arts festival, with a feast at its centre. She uses local produce, draws on local gastronomic talent and takes inspiration from the spirit of Northern Soul to create the banquet

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Short
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers hop on their motorbikes for a culinary voyage of discovery along the Namibian coastline. They set up a traditional barbecue known as a braai in the Germanic town of Swakopmund, finding the best spot to cook among the dunes by taking to the air in a microlight

programme.title: What's Up, Doc? (1972)
programme.desc: A timid professor in San Francisco for a conference bumps into an eccentric, disaster-prone woman whose antics throw his carefully ordered life into upheaval - while a case of mistaken identity involving a jewel thief and a government whistle-blower only adds to his woes. Comedy, starring Barbra Streisand, Ryan O'Neal and Madeline Kahn

programme.title: Some Like It Hot (1959)
programme.desc: After witnessing a Mob hit in Chicago, two male jazz musicians don drag and hide out with an all-girl band to evade the gangsters pursuing them. However, the pair struggle to disguise their attraction for the beautiful lead singer - while one of them catches the eye of a smitten millionaire. Billy Wilder's classic romantic comedy, starring Jack Lemmon, Tony Curtis and Marilyn Monroe

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: As milk production deadline day looms, Lucy and Adam race to finish the shed, buy more dairy cows and get them used to a new milking robot system. Callum and Zara have had a few difficult years since arriving on Arran, but their fortunes are at last turning as they build a future for their family. After a busy year Andrew and Gayle hope their farming enterprises continue to grow, and that grandad Terrence can take a step back

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: South Wales: A Critical Season in the Forest
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Sammi Kinghorn explore the forests of the Welsh valleys at a critical time. with wildfires posing a growing threat as spring gives way to summer. They meet people shaping the future of these landscapes, from fire prevention and recreation to conservation and training the next generation. Meanwhile, Tom Heap heads to Devon and Cornwall to investigate the state of the UK's seaweed farming industry

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents the results and this afternoon's news from the final round of Premier League fixtures

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents the show from London Zoo in Regent's Park, with Will Axon and Thomas Plant doing their valuations al fresco

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve joins forces with ropes expert Aldo Kane and Saudi Arabian geologists Mahmoud Al-Shanti and Hala Alwagdani to explore a volcanic underworld in search of the longest lava tube in the Arabian Peninsula. Geological evidence suggests that at least 400km of tunnels lie beneath the Harrat Khaybar lava field, subterranean passageways that could contain bones and artefacts from ancient civilisations. Only 25km of these lava tubes have ever been explored - until now

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: Part one of two. The historian examines the turning points that led to America's declaration of independence from Britain in 1776 and asks whether this explosive split could have been avoided. The eight-year war that followed claimed thousands of lives and ended in a humiliating defeat for George III, and Lucy sets out to uncover why America chose this path and how Britain's missteps fractured an empire and shook the monarchy

programme.title: Scandinavia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: On the last leg of his Scandinavian journey, Simon travels through the pine forests of Sweden to Copenhagen, the historic capital of Denmark. He discovers that all is not quite as rosy as it seems and uncovers surprising and sometimes shocking sides of Sweden and Denmark, two of the happiest, wealthiest and most equal countries on Earth

programme.title: Later... with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Taking to the stage at Alexandra Palace Theatre in north London is musician and producer James Blake, who performs two songs from his recent album Trying. Also appearing are Amercian blues rock musician Joe Bonamassa, singer-songwriter Sekou, US country star Ashley McBryde, and noise rock band Mandy, Indiana. Veteran American singer Jocelyn chats to Jools at the piano about her musical journey

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy makers on the key issues

programme.title: Radio 1's Big Weekend
programme.desc: The Brit and Grammy-winning singer-songwriter's headline set on the main stage at Herrington Country Park, which brought the festival to a close

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: After racing over 11,000km through Europe and Asia, the teams face one final test of endurance as they embark on the last leg of their journey to the finish line in Hatgal, Mongolia, where the first team to arrive will claim victory and a £20,000 prize

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Tom Allen is the host for more stand-up showcases performed in front of a live audience at London's Hammersmith Apollo. Rosie Jones and Kae Kurd take to the stage, and other performers set to appear in this series include Sindhu Vee, Alex Edelman, Lou Sanders, Darren Harriott, Jessica Fostekew, Stephen Bailey, Desiree Burch, Paul McCaffrey, Jonny Pelham, Ardal O'Hanlon, Sara Barron, Mawaan Rizwan, Guz Khan, Kiri Pritchard-McLean, Rhys James, Sarah Pascoe, Chris McCausland and Flo & Joan

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The naturalist and explorer ventures into more uncharted territory in pursuit of new discoveries and world firsts, beginning by heading to the Kamchatka Peninsula in the far east of Russia. He joins a team of world-class kayakers to take on the first descent of the Kronotsky River, setting out to paddle from source to sea, starting in the shadow of a still-active volcano and ending at the Pacific Ocean

programme.title: The Cage
programme.desc: Matty and Leanne need to pull off one last roll of the dice as hostile forces close in and the evidence stacks up against them

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: TV presenter and keen gardener Katie Piper joins Diarmuid and Carol to visit three more gardens. The judges head to Colin's surprising rustic garden in suburban Ballymena, before seeing how Adrienne has transformed two acres near the windswept Glenshane Pass in the foothills of the Sperrin Mountains. Their final stop is in Dromore, Co Down, where Julietta has created a garden designed with her young family in mind, including a wild meadow playground and a courtyard filled with edible plants

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Roman Kemp meets a Glasgow children's entertainer who is desperate for a good night's sleep. Adding a hot bath to an evening routine has been shown to help improve sleep, reduce the risk of heart disease and even burn a few extra calories

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Kelly Cates is joined by Alan Shearer and Wayne Rooney for action from the final Premier League matches of the season, including Crystal Palace v Arsenal at Selhurst Park and Manchester City v Aston Villa at Etihad Stadium. The title race between Arsenal and City had been expected to go to the wire, with the Gunners looking to win the league for the first time since 2004, and the Citizens looking to claim the Premier for the seventh time under Pep Guardiola. Plus, Tottenham Hotspur v Everton, West Ham United v Leeds United, Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester United, Burnley v Wolverhampton Wanderers, Fulham v Newcastle United, Liverpool v Brentford, Nottingham Forest v Bournemouth and Sunderland v Chelsea

programme.title: BBC News

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: ISS UFO Swarm, Yale Lake Leaf Monster and Rat King

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Witch's Broomstick, UFOs vs Volcanoes and Oakville Blobs
programme.desc: An examination of several seemingly impossible things 'caught on film', including a witch's broomstick 'floating' in mid-air and fossilised footprints found in Texas. Plus, a look at what might have caused gelatinous blobs of rain to fall from the sky and UFOs purportedly using volcanoes as hidden bases of operations

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Following investigators as they seek new clues regarding the Malaysia Airlines flight MH370 that disappeared in March 2014. Plus, the astronaut who spotted something strange in the Sahara Desert

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.sub-title: Lost Treasure of the Grand Canyon
programme.desc: Strange spots near the Grand Canyon may lead to the legendary treasure of a notorious bandit. Plus, a massive chunk of ice disappears in Antarctica

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Strange black holes appear near Egypt's pyramids, ruins in Peru suggest the Incas knew more than may be imagined, and a mysterious red swarm invades a beach in India

programme.title: Secrets of the Lost Liners
programme.desc: The ocean liner Titanic sank on its maiden voyage from Southampton to New York in April 1912. The collision with an iceberg in the North Atlantic led to the loss of more than 1,500 lives. Soon the First World War overshadowed the tragedy, and she almost vanished from memory. Decades later, two very different men had dreams and ambitions to re-discover her

programme.title: Secrets of the Lost Liners
programme.desc: While not an ocean liner, Costa Concordia, like all other cruise ships of today, owed her DNA to the earlier vessels that helped globalise the world. In January 2012, she suffered a gash on the port side of her hull and ran aground on her side leaving 32 people dead.

programme.title: Hazardous History with Henry Winkler
programme.desc: A nostalgic journey through America's past reveals a time when any and all cures could be advertised and sold to the public indiscriminately, without any redress being offered when they failed. Henry dives into a world where animal skin could replace one's own and where doctors gave coffee enemas

programme.title: Hitler's Handmaidens
programme.desc: A modern examination of the Third Reich reflects a genocidal party that could not have achieved power without the support of wealthy and influential women, and one that could not have carried out its atrocities without such willing accomplices. This programme looks at the women who surrounded Hitler after he came to power and how they were held up as an Aryan ideal for German society to strive for once the Nazi dictator approved of their positions

programme.title: Hitler's Secret Sex Life
programme.desc: The myriad of rumours, theories and disputed historical accounts of Adolf Hitler's sexual psychology, exploring the impact of his alleged and proven sex life on his behaviour. This episode delves into the truth behind the rumours of Hitler's sex life, including whispers of homosexuality, voyeurism and sadomasochism

programme.title: Weird Britain

programme.title: What on Earth?

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns

She discovers the secrets of the city's cathedral, a centre of ecclesiastical power and also the site of the shocking murder of Thomas Becket. Comedy starring Colin Firth, Ruth Gemmell, Neil Pearson, Holly Aird and Mark Strong

programme.title: Taskmaster

Meanwhile, Greg Davies proves that he's a responsible master of his loveable pet, Little Alex Horne

programme.title: Formula 1
programme.sub-title: Canadian Grand Prix Sprint Qualifying Highlights 2026
programme.desc: Action from the battle for sprint race positions in the fifth round of the season, held at Circuit Gilles Villeneuve in Montreal

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Mojito, a Cuban cafe in Brooklyn, New York. The restaurant was the shared dream of two newlyweds who have separated since its opening, and whose constant public quarrelling is distracting staff and driving customers away

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Joe Lycett is joined by comedian and actress Rose Matafeo for a two-day stay in Sweden's third city, Malmö. After checking into their circus-themed hotel, the pair make use of the complimentary bikes to discover the city. Attractions include The Disgusting Food Museum, where the tickets double as sick bags as they challenge the notion of what is and isn't edible. Next day, the duo appreciate the 19th-century splendour of the Ribersborg Kallbadhus, enjoying a sauna fand an icy dip in a seawater pool. They then hotfoot it to Folkets park for a round of Malmö-themed mini golf

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Four sets of campsite proprietors battle it out to be named best value, with the competition beginning at Amber's Bell Tent Camping in Mannington, Norfolk, a retro-styled pre-erected campsite where artistic host Amber Wykes challenges her guests to go native with a series of bushcraft trials. Over dinner, guests Derek and Julie's opinions on glamping irritate both Amber, and glampsite owners Nick and Sheila. Then back at the tents, brother-and-sister team Russell and Jane aren't too impressed with the sleeping arrangements. In the morning, the self-catering breakfast leads to complaints aimed squarely at Amber

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second day of the competition is at Park Farm Camping and Caravan Site in Norfolk, where husband and wife team Derek and Julie Nice are proud to show off their budget, family-friendly site. Former marksman Derek treats his guests to a spot of clay pigeon shooting, bringing out his competitive side

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to 500-year-old Layer Marney Tower in Essex for the third day of the competition, where they are welcomed into luxury glamping lodges by Nick and Sheila Charrington. The guests have little to complain about with the accommodation, but a revelation over dinner changes how they see things. Plus, a treasure hunt organised by the hosts sends one team on a wild goose chase

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Day four is at the Foxhunter Park in Kent, where brother and sister Jane Stacey and Russell Saunders own a caravan site that includes a bistro and entertainment. The accommodation is luxurious but the large scale of the site does not sit well with the campers - and the evening entertainment of a fire-eating limbo dancer leaves most of them cold

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The campers meet for the final time to find out what they thought one another's stay was worth. Amber is offended by Derek's low scoring, while he is thrown by Nick and Sheila's feedback. Russell and Jane have an axe to grind with Derek whose low scoring of their park is made worse by what they see as a personal attack on their hosting, while Nick and Sheila appear to emerge relatively unscathed

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: Matt and Lorraine plan to restore a dilapidated outbuilding and turn it into guest accommodation

programme.title: The King of Queens

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie goes against Doug's wishes by asking his ex-girlfriend out to lunch in an effort to befriend her, while Spence agrees to go on a date with a man to boost his morale. Guest starring Janeane Garofalo, with Leah Remini and Patton Oswalt

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug goes into hospital to have his appendix removed - but while sedated, he unwittingly reveals a catalogue of sexual fantasies, none of which involve Carrie

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Arthur sells his burial plot to Spence's mother. Doug is unnerved by a customer's tendency to answer the door half-naked, and Carrie gets a new job as a dog walker

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: As a challenge, Ray writes a moving eulogy for Frank - despite the obvious fact he is still alive. Ray Romano and Peter Boyle star

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray thinks something is wrong and confides in Debra when he has a miserable night with his friends, but an invitation to join Frank for a steam bath restores his spirits. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Peter Boyle

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra give Robert $1,000 to help him out of dire financial straits - but he takes advantage of their generosity by jetting off for a holiday in Las Vegas

programme.title: Frasier

Comedy, guest starring Megan Mullally (Will & Grace) and Lisa Darr (Ellen), with Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart begins spying on the neighbours through Lisa's telescope while recovering from an injury, and is alarmed to see something that convinces him Ned Flanders has murdered his wife

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Marge buy a self-help video to improve their lives - but make the mistake of enlisting Bart and Lisa's assistance. Featuring the voices of James Brown and Albert Brooks

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Nasa decides to send an average American into space as a publicity stunt - but the chances of success are jeopardised when Homer is selected for the honour. Featuring the guest voices of Buzz Aldrin and James Taylor

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Sideshow Bob is pardoned for his crimes and decides to run for political office - but still displays a few distinctly criminal tendencies. Featuring the guest voices of Kelsey Grammer and Larry King

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: This week's competition kicks off at Rosaire Guest House in Llandudno, where host Jo and her mum Pat are going for a home-from-home vibe

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

At first, their guests struggle to find fault - until Pat discovers that standards in the bathroom may have slipped. Things get prickly during a visit to a botanical garden in the afternoon but it's at feedback time that the hosts have the biggest thorn in their sides

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's final visit is to Hideaway Escapes in Pembrokeshire, where sisters Lesley and Sarah are looking to inject a bit of colour into everyone's lives. The idea of getting back to nature bugs half of the B&Bers - and things get spicy when one set of guests refuse their room and take refuge in the car instead, leaving Lesley and Sarah all revved up come feedback time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: With facility failings, bathroom blues and even one set of B&Bers refusing to even try out one of their rivals' rooms all on the agenda, Payment Day is set to be a feisty occasion. It remains to be seen who will emerge victorious when the dust settles

programme.title: Jamie's Ultimate BBQ
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver cooks his barbecue breakfast, which takes a much-loved classic to smoky heights, then serves up his smoke-kissed sesame chicken burger, accompanied by crunchy slaw and Korean-inspired sauce. His veggie barbecue platter offers the perfect spread, comprising smoky baba ghanoush, dressed roasted peppers, minty courgettes and halloumi cheese with apricots, then finishes off with his surf 'n' turf mixed grill, cooked on a plancha griddle with butter and wine

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Mutant Mayhem (2023)
programme.desc: Turtle brothers Donatello, Leonardo, Michelangelo and Raphael have been raised in the sewers of New York by their mentor Splinter. The wise rat has sheltered his four wards from the cruelty of the human world but the brothers yearn to be accepted as normal teenagers by New Yorkers. They hope to win human hearts by performing selfless, heroic deeds with the help of their new friend April O'Neil. Animated fantasy, with the voices of Nicolas Cantu and Jackie Chan

programme.title: Steam Train Diaries
programme.desc: The steam engine Tornado travels from the remote Levisham station to the market town of Pickering. With the season in full swing, disaster strikes as a giant wildfire jeopardises the steam train services. Volunteers work tirelessly to keep the stations spick and span, and guide dog puppies gaining their first experience of trains

programme.title: World's Most Secret Hotels
programme.desc: In the Bradshaw Mountains in Arizona, USA, hidden down an 11-kilometre dirt road, is a luxury hotel built around a geothermal wonder. At The Bus Stop in Scotland, there are nine luxury buses for hire, while a secret retreat in Poland has been designed to look like an open chest of drawers. Plus, a one-of-a-kind glamping pod in eastern England, and a boutique bolthole inside an ex-windmill in Suffolk

programme.title: The Equalizer 3 (2023)

Thriller, starring Denzel Washington and Dakota Fanning

With a Cuban chef cooking Nordic cuisine and a filthy kitchen, things go from bad to worse until it all comes to a head on relaunch night

programme.desc: In Bournemouth, Dorset, PR company owners Jackie and Richard are first to host, putting on a classy evening with a few celebrity names thrown in for good measure. programme.desc: Carrie persuades Deacon and Kelly to go with her to see Doug's parents in Florida - but their hotel proves to be overcrowded with spring holidaymakers, ruining her plans for a perfect holiday. Starring Leah Remini

programme.desc: Marge sees The Bridges of Madison County and begins pining for a little romance to spice up her life, so the family reminisces about impassioned encounters in days gone by. Featuring the guest voices of Michelle Pfeiffer and Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first destination of the week is the Golden Grove Retreat in Carmarthenshire, featuring an afternoon of yoga and a healthy breakfast that leaves guests pining for a fry-up

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rivals arrive at Rosita Guest House in Weston-super-Mare, where hosts Sheila and Phil dream of perfect scores. Things get off to a great start - until unexpected items turn up

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.sub-title: King Square Apartments in Bridgwater, Somerset
programme.desc: The third visit is to King Square Apartments in Bridgwater, Somerset. Pam and Mya hope their self-catering digs will do the business, and the guests are certainly wowed by the building - but not the princely price tag

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's final visit is to Hartridge Springs in Upottery in Devon, where Anton and Emma hope their upmarket glampsite will bring a touch of opulence to the Great Outdoors. However, with some first-time campers among their guests, they've got their work cut out. The B&Bers try their best not to fall asleep while Anton talks about fossils on an open-top tram. At breakfast, the guests find out, it's quite hard to cook a fry-up without a frying pan

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment Day finds the B&Bers champing at the bit to tear some shreds off each other. Rob and Louise want to straighten out Anton's bed bother, while Pam and Mya prepare to give some cutting feedback about knives that aren't sharp enough. After everyone's had their say, the week's winners are revealed

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help Karol and Richard with their tired and impractical three-bedroom bungalow in St Osyth, Essex. They bought their home in 2022 after Richard's long-term prostate cancer diagnosis, hoping that single-storey living would ease future care but the practical plan has sparked tension. Karol is ready to list it. She's fed up with the dated décor, the tiny kitchen that can't fit the washing machine, and the single bathroom. But Richard is convinced that these issues can be solved. He would rather use the £22,000 potential moving costs to improve their house, but after 18 months of stasis, he needs Kirstie's help to find a way forward. Working with a £60,000 Love It budget, Kirstie proposes an internal reconfiguration to bring balance back to the home. But with Richard adjusting her plans from the beginning, she must draw on all her expertise to keep the project on course. Meanwhile, Phil faces his own challenges, finding sun-kissed gardens to satisfy Karol and enough kitchen storage for Richard, all within his £525,000 List It budget

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: A couple attempt to combine an army lorry and an old caravan to create the ultimate off-roader, and a man returns to his family's farm to build a luxury cabin. Meanwhile, George visits a building in Denmark that was designed to be a work of art and an office, checks out a skip that's been turned into a home, and takes inspiration for his scissor lift cabin from a stunning 18th-century bridge

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer

programme.title: Cruising to the Ends of the Earth

programme.title: Vengeance: Murder on the Heath
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part fact-based crime drama. Best friends Gagandip and Ravi fell in love with student doctor Mundill, a love triangle that destroyed their friendship and ultimately ended in what the headlines called the honeytrap murder. Gagandip attempted to sexually assault Mundill, who conspired with Ravi on a revenge plot that went horribly wrong. Starring Dee Ahluwalia, Ikky Kabir and Sasha Desouza-Willock
