programme.title: Gabby's Dollhouse: The Movie (2025)
programme.desc: When Gabby's prized dollhouse ends up with eccentric cat lady Vera, the youngster embarks on a adventure to reunite the Gabby Cats and retrieve her beloved diorama before it's too late. Comedy adventure, starring Laila Lockhart Kraner and Kristen Wiig

programme.title: Wuthering Heights (2026)
programme.desc: Catherine Earnshaw is raised by her alcoholic father at a Yorkshire farmhouse, with Nelly Dean as a constant companion. Mr Earnshaw 'acquires' an orphaned boy named Heathcliff during one of his gallivants and gifts the waif to Cathy as her pet. The children become inseparable, and as adults they embark on a destructive relationship. Writer-director Emerald Fennell's drama based on Emily Bronte's romance, starring Margot Robbie, Jacob Elordi and Martin Clunes

programme.title: The Bride! (2026)
programme.desc: In 1930s Chicago, maverick scientist Dr Euphronius gives life to a murdered woman, sparking romance, police interest and radical change. Writer/director Maggie Gyllenhaal's horror based on The Bride of Frankenstein, starring Jessie Buckley, Christian Bale and Annette Bening

Director Edgar Wright's comedy thriller based on the Stephen King story, starring Glen Powell, Lee Pace and Josh Brolin

programme.title: Spinal Tap II: The End Continues (2025)
programme.desc: Nigel Tufnel, David St Hubbins and Derek Smalls reunite after a 15-year break for one final concert. Their progress is charted once more by documentary maker Marti DiBergi, who offers perhaps too much perspective on their new direction. Comedy sequel, written by and starring Michael McKean, Harry Shearer, Christopher Guest and director Rob Reiner, in his final film. Kerry Godliman and Chris Addison also star with cameos by Paul McCartney, Elton John and Garth Brooks

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The crimes of American serial killer Dennis Rader, who murdered 10 people in Kansas area over 17 years and dubbed himself BTK - meaning 'Bind, Torture, Kill'

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: An investigation into the murderous mother and son Sante and Kenneth Kimes, who killed their elderly landlady in a greed-fuelled ploy

programme.title: Murder Uncut
programme.desc: Letecia Stauch reported that her 11-year-old stepson hadn't come home, but what followed was a trial of deceit, secrets and murder

programme.title: Murders That Shocked the Nation
programme.desc: Fred Dinenage examines the case of John Christie, who murdered six women between 1943 and 1953 and hid their bodies in his house at 10 Rillington Place in Notting Hill, London

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of Malcolm Green, who was jailed for life for the brutal murder of a Cardiff prostitute, only to kill again when he was released after 18 years

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: London's Metropolitan Police dog unit tests the skills of its leading specialist search dogs, pitting spaniels Diesel and Billy against each other as they hunt for hidden heroin. On the streets of north London, shift leader Pete Madden puts his Alsatian Brodie in the line of fire when they target two armed robbers, but as the trail runs cold they find themselves in pursuit of a gang of car vandals. Ken Stott narrates

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness the best and worst behaviour on the nation's busiest motorway

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness the best and worst behaviour on the nation's busiest motorway

programme.title: Stop, Search, Seize
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of Ireland's border force officers as they fight to stop gangs of organised smugglers from bringing drugs, money, guns, cars and even exotic animals into the country

programme.title: UK Border Force
programme.desc: Officers search UK-bound lorries and discover illegal immigrants during rush hour in Calais, while police investigate the workforce of a chicken factory

programme.title: Road Wars
programme.desc: Police officers use unmarked cars fitted with on-board video cameras, offering an insight into vehicle crime and how it can be prevented. The programme also features footage of dangerous drivers

programme.title: Caught on Dashcam
programme.desc: Fly-on-the-dashboard documentary showcasing outrageous road disasters, captured in video form by drivers' in-vehicle dashboard cameras

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A customer thrown out of a bar takes revenge by driving into a group of locals outside, and the owner of a restaurant catches a group planning to leave without paying

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A shop owner grapples with a dangerous gunman as his two daughters watch in horror, and a frail 93-year-old has money stolen, but a large number of visitors could be the culprit

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Local people join forces with the police to help catch a thief when a pub landlady has money stolen that was meant for her seriously ill daughter. Dominic Littlewood presents

programme.title: Forensics: Catching the Killer
programme.desc: When the body of a Lithuanian immigrant is discovered, detectives are faced with uncovering how she died and who is responsible for her death

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The little-known case of Ronald Dominique, a serial killer and rapist who murdered at least 23 men and boys and was known as the Bayou Strangler

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of Paul Michael Stephani, aka the Weepy Voiced Killer, who would whimper confessions over the phone after each of his murders

programme.title: How I Caught the Killer
programme.sub-title: Tears Of A Clown - Gordon Wardell
programme.desc: After Gordon Wardell makes an emotional appeal to find his wife's killers, police soon realise they must look closer to home

programme.title: How I Caught the Killer
programme.desc: Investigating the murder of Brian McKandie. Initially deemed accidental, the handyman's death was soon considered murder due to his severe injuries

programme.title: The Suzy Lamplugh Mystery
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary exploring one of the most baffling cases in recent history - the mysterious disappearance of estate agent Suzy Lamplugh from Fulham, south west London. The last clue to her whereabouts was an appointment in July 1986 to show a house to someone she referred to as 'Mr Kipper'

programme.title: The Suzy Lamplugh Mystery
programme.desc: Part two of two. Documentary exploring one of the most baffling cases in recent history - the mysterious 1986 disappearance of the London estate agent. With no body, Suzi was officially declared dead, presumed murdered, in 1994

programme.title: Forensics: Catching the Killer
programme.desc: Feature-length special about the discovery of two murder victims in Pembrokeshire in 1989. The answers to their demise lay in the forensics, as a shocking truth was revealed

programme.title: Premier League 360 - Big Sam's Bolton
programme.desc: A look at Sam Allardyce's iconic Bolton team of the early 2000s in the Premier League

programme.title: Terry Venables: A Man Can Dream
programme.desc: A look at the life and times of the football player-turned-manager, who died in November 2023, intertwined with the story of England's unforgettable run at Euro 96, with insight from players, friends and family

programme.title: Bobby Robson: More Than a Manager (2018)
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the career of the much-loved former England, Barcelona and Newcastle United manager. Via the Hand of God and Paul Gascoigne's tears, to titles across Europe, Robson overcame the most extreme challenges - including fighting for his life after being diagnosed with cancer - before returning to football to manage his beloved Newcastle

programme.title: Gascoigne (2015)
programme.desc: Documentary about Paul Gascoigne, which charts his rise to fame from his humble roots in the North East to the world stage and his subsequent personal problems. The film features an extensive interview with the troubled former footballing superstar, as well as comment from those who admired his skills, including Wayne Rooney, Jose Mourinho and Gazza's ex-England and Tottenham Hotspur team-mate Gary Lineker

programme.title: My Icon
programme.desc: The football manager speaks about the people that inspired his career

programme.title: Spitfire (2018)
programme.desc: The story of the British single-seat fighter plane the Supermarine Spitfire, designed by RJ Mitchell and famous for its role in the Battle of Britain. Archive footage, new film of the aircraft in flight and interviews with the last surviving veterans who flew the plane in combat combine to tell its story

programme.title: The Wimbledon Kidnapping
programme.desc: A look back at the mysterious disappearance of Muriel McKay in Wimbledon in 1969, as well as the trial of the first kidnap and ransom case in Britain

programme.title: The Yogurt Shop Murders
programme.desc: A look at the conclusion to the case of the killing of four teenage girls in 1991, when Austin police convened a press conference to announce a break in the investigation just three weeks after the documentary concluded

programme.title: David Fuller: Monster in the Morgue
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary about the case of David Fuller, a murderer who also sexually abused the bodies of more than 100 women and girls in a hospital mortuary

programme.title: The Armstrong Lie (2014)
programme.desc: Documentary following cycling champion Lance Armstrong's fall from grace. Film-maker Alex Gibney was initially commissioned to follow the cyclist as he prepared to make a professional comeback by competing in the Tour de France, only to find himself documenting an unprecedented scandal as Armstrong was exposed as having used performance-enhancing drugs

programme.title: Dogtown and Z-Boys
programme.desc: Documentary charting the history of skateboarding, from its 1970s emergence as a land-based form of surfing in Los Angeles and its subsequent explosion of popularity worldwide, to its current status as a widely-practised extreme sport. Narrated by Sean Penn, with contributions from experts Jay Adams and Tony Alva

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of British director J Lee Thompson, whose films include Yield to the Night, Ice Cold in Alex, Cape Fear and The Guns of Navarone

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of British actor Robert Shaw. Often cast in menacing roles, he is widely remembered for his performance as shark hunter Quint in Jaws

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the English actor, who won an Oscar for his role as Professor Henry Higgins in the 1964 musical My Fair Lady

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of John Carpenter, known for his cult classics such as Dark Star, The Thing and Halloween

programme.title: Enigma
programme.desc: Documentary featuring an exploration of transgender identity and legacy through comparing and contrasting the life stories of April Ashley and Amanda Lear

programme.title: History of the Sitcom
programme.desc: A look at the development of situation comedies, tracing their evolution from the 1950s to the 21st century, beginning by looking at the depictions of families

programme.title: History of the Sitcom
programme.desc: A look at comedy shows set in the workplace, which offer insights into the state of the American workforce

programme.title: History of the Sitcom
programme.desc: A look at comedy shows that revolve around friendships, exploring TV's challenging quest for inclusivity and authentic representation

programme.title: History of the Sitcom
programme.desc: A look at the pioneering comedy shows that tackled race and diversity, helping pave the way for progress with hilarity and laughter

programme.title: I Am Richard Pryor
programme.desc: Documentary reflecting on the highs and lows of the comedian and actor's life, featuring interviews with Mike Epps, Tiffany Haddish and his widow Jennifer Lee

programme.title: My Icon
programme.desc: The former sprint superstar and Olympic gold medalist discusses the individuals that have inspired him in his life and career

programme.title: Hitsville: The Making of Motown (2019)
programme.desc: Rare performances, interviews and rarely seen footage come together to paint a picture of the history and origins of the Motown record label

programme.title: How the Beatles Changed the World
programme.desc: Through exclusive interviews and a wealth of rarely seen footage, this film reveals the previously untold story of how four young men from Liverpool transformed the world for ever

programme.title: I Am Johnny Cash
programme.desc: A celebration of the country singer-songwriter built around 12 of his classic tracks spanning four decades, and featuring interviews with his family and friends. Featuring contributions from Sheryl Crow, Kris Kristofferson, Willie Nelson, John Mellencamp and Kid Rock

programme.title: Mr Dynamite: The Rise of James Brown
programme.desc: The career of the singer, musician and producer, charting his journey from his first hit Please, Please, Please in 1956 through his pioneering funk phase to later chart success

programme.title: Music Box: Counting Crows
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how the rock band and singer Adam Duritz navigated sudden fame while staying true to their art after the success of their debut album

programme.title: Searching for Sugar Man (2012)
programme.desc: Documentary following two fans in search of their musical idol. American singer Sixto Rodriguez enjoyed a brief recording career in the 1970s before fading into obscurity. However, his politically conscious music became incredibly popular in South Africa. Bizarre stories of his death had circulated over the years, but two devotees plan to find out what really became of him

programme.title: Chernobyl: My Promised Land
programme.desc: Eastern Ukrainian refugees find themselves unwelcome in the village of Dytyatky near Chernobyl, with locals sceptical about their leader's madcap business ventures

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of Rob Reiner, whose films include This Is Spinal Tap, Stand by Me, Misery, When Harry Met Sally and the Oscar-nominated A Few Good Men

programme.title: 100 Foot Wave
programme.desc: When another WSL contest in Nazaré is announced, Tony finally gets an opportunity to compete

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A profile of the Russian-born actor, who won an Oscar for The King and I and also starred in The Ten Commandments, The Magnificent Seven and Westworld

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of Steven Spielberg, whose films include Jaws, ET the Extra-Terrestrial, Raiders of the Lost Ark and Jurassic Park and who won Oscars for directing Schindler's List and Saving Private Ryan

programme.title: I'm Chevy Chase and You're Not
programme.desc: Chevy Chase was a cast member on the inaugural series of Saturday Night Live and he built on that success to star in numerous Hollywood comedies and host the Academy Awards. He played hapless father Clark Griswold in the National Lampoon films and ran amok in pictures including Caddyshack, Spies Like Us, Fletch and Three Amigos. Two-time Emmy Award winner Marina Zenovich directs an authorised feature-length documentary portrait of Chase, which includes rare and intimate home movies and archive footage. Chase and his family share memories directly to camera, enriched with contributions from friends and collaborators including Dan Aykroyd, Beverly D'Angelo, Goldie Hawn, Lorne Michaels, Ryan Reynolds and Martin Short.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens

programme.title: UFO Hunters

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: Beau Ouimette, Rick Edwards and Gary Bankhead travel to Colchester, Essex, to find evidence from the second English Civil War in what became the longest siege in British history. Finds include a short-barreled Carbine gun which was commonly used by the Cavalry in the 1600s and a horse stirrup believed to be from the 1648 siege due to it's age and position on the river bank.

programme.title: Cracking the Code

programme.title: U-Boat Wargamers
programme.desc: The remarkable story of Gilbert Roberts, Jean Laidlaw and the Women's Royal Navy Service (WRNS) - aka the 'Wrens'. programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.sub-title: A Roman Mystery and Waterloo's Disappearing Dead
programme.desc: Alice Roberts meets an archaeology team in Lincoln who are looking for more evidence about a Roman occupation along a road that leaves the city. She also visits Norfolk to explore an Iron Age hillfort, discovering how 4th-century Romans used it despite being built in the Iron Age. At a small community dig in Lincolnshire, Alice learns about a newly found object - a Roman dodecahedron, and discusses its possible functions with a renowned expert in the digging tent

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits the south of England, discovering Britain's oldest shoe through a popular form of archaeology - mud larking. While in Kent, Alice visits a dig where one of the largest and oldest stone hand axes in the UK has been uncovered. Another archaeological dig reveals Henry V's lost shipyard on the south coast, and in Dover, Alice learns about top-secret Second World War defences from Jon Barker as he investigates the formerly classified weapons as well as the site where they were found at

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores archaeological treasures in the west of Britain, beginning with a look at the remains of Snodhill Castle, a little-known ruin on the crest of a hill in Herefordshire. programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.desc: In 1592, Elizabeth I visited Sudeley Castle in the Cotswolds for a three-day party commemorating the nation's victory over the Spanish Armada. Here, Raksha Dave, Alex Langlands and assorted volunteers and archaeologists hunt for a lost garden planted as part of the celebrations, while there is also a visit to Kenilworth Castle to investigate an Elizabethan garden still in full bloom today. Plus, a look at how the garden's Tudor owner, Robert Dudley transformed his estate to win favour with his Queen

programme.desc: Tori Herridge and a team of scientists attempt to piece together the lives behind discovered bones, and find out what happened to these people and how they died. This episode comes from Leeds, where remains of children and teenagers have been uncovered beneath a shopping arcade. Analysis of the broken bones reveals that they date back to the industrial revolution, and a thorough examination of the remains provides a detailed picture of the lives these young people would have lived during a tumultuous period of British history

programme.title: Alien Corridors

programme.title: Killer Kings
programme.desc: The Roman Emperor Caligula, also known as Gaius, exploring his brief but brutal reign from AD 37 to 41, highlighting his initial popular policies - cutting taxes and pardoning exiles - which were followed by a descent into madness and tyrannical behaviour. This documentary delves into the historical context of his rule and the events surrounding his assassination

programme.title: Nazi Murder Mysteries

programme.desc: Hitler's government was firmly in the saddle in October 1934 and its anti-Jewish policy was supported by millions of Germans. Openly and publicly, Jews were discriminated against, disenfranchised, and persecuted. The indifference of many countries towards those Jewish refugees forced to leave Germany was evident

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: In January 2008, a shadow was cast over the town of Stephenville, Texas, when a UFO passed overhead at supersonic speed. The event was witnessed by scores of stunned locals who remain desperate for answers on what exactly they saw that winter's evening. Craig and space journalist Sarah Cruddas talk to eyewitness private pilot Steve Allen who was with friends when he saw a vast UFO passing overhead at a speed estimated to be 2,000mph. He describes the craft as having an array of bright, high-intensity lights at its rear - and pursued by two US Air Force F16 fighter jets. The team discovers that the US military, who initially denied any knowledge of this event, changed their story in the weeks following the incident, adding fuel to the theory that there could be a high-level cover-up

programme.title: Conspiracy Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: A look at the places Hitler and members of his inner circle could have fled to, should the theories about them surviving the fall of Berlin be valid

programme.title: Ancient Aliens

programme.title: The Curse of Oak Island
programme.desc: Documentary following brothers Marty and Rick Lagina as they search for the mysterious treasure buried on a Nova Scotia island, which is believed to be protected by a deadly curse

programme.title: How Disney Built America
programme.desc: The story of animation film producer Walt Disney and how the iconic Disney brand he established continues to shape generations as a cultural and worldwide phenomenon. The story starts with Walt in his early days in Hollywood, when he quickly lost his first successful cartoon character to a powerful middleman. This opening episode also focuses on the birth of iconic character Mickey Mouse and how he began as a pencil sketch and rocketed to lasting fame

programme.title: How Disney Built America
programme.desc: How, while facing soaring costs, mounting criticism and a tight deadline, film producer Walt Disney managed to create his first full-length feature film - 1937's Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs. This spearheaded a rise of the billion-dollar business of animated movies

programme.title: How Disney Built America
programme.desc: The obstacles Walt Disney overcame to create his company's first theme park. Instead of just thrill rides, the visitor enjoyed an immersive journey into a story, akin to being inside a movie

programme.title: How Disney Built America
programme.desc: Amid financial troubles, Disney hired an ingenious marketing whiz who helped them figure out how to bring their beloved characters off the screen and into the hands of millions. Mickey Mouse became the first celebrity face to grace a cereal box and from that moment, the licensing of Disney products becomes a lucrative industry and the gold standard for multimedia corporations all over the world

programme.title: How Disney Built America
programme.desc: The construction of Walt Disney World Resort in Florida, which transformed 27,000 acres of swampland into the world's largest theme park. The project needed to oversome construction delays, personal tragedy and secrecy, as 'Project X'

programme.title: How Disney Built America

programme.desc: Alice Roberts travels to Bamburgh, Northumberland, to learn about the generations of Anglo Saxons who lived and worked there, gaining an insight into the illnesses they suffered and the societies in which they lived. programme.title: The Lost Women Spies
programme.desc: Assigned to the British War Crime Investigation Unit in post-war Germany, British intelligence officer Vera Atkins pursued her agents' fate at Natzweiler and Ravensbrück. Piecing together testimonies, she insisted on obtaining official records to substantiate her findings for upcoming trials. While Vera sought justice for her girls, a letter challenged her verdicts on Noor?s fate. Suspicions of double agents led Vera to hunt down a Nazi mastermind. She was still to determine whether any agents had escapeed, and if not who had betrayed them

programme.title: The Lost Women Spies
programme.desc: How the capture of a key Nazi uncovered shocking realities. Amid demands for accountability, spymistress Vera Atkins and her team had to navigate murky waters to confront uncomfortable truths

programme.title: David Attenborough's First Life
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part documentary, the naturalist explores the origins of life on Earth. Beginning the journey near his childhood home in Charnwood Forest, Leicestershire, he learns how a fossil discovery transformed scientists' understanding of the evolution of complex organisms, and in Newfoundland and Australia he reveals the earliest kinds of animal to exist on the planet. With the help of cutting-edge technology and visual effects, the programme brings the creatures to life, demonstrating how they survived

programme.title: David Attenborough's First Life
programme.desc: In the conclusion of the two-part documentary, the naturalist travels to Burgess Shale in Canada's Rocky Mountains, where he explores how evolution gained pace once creatures developed mouths and mobility. In Scotland, he reveals that one of the smallest fossils found holds the secret to how animals began surviving on dry land, and explains the bloom of life during the oxygen-rich Carboniferous period

programme.title: Riverdance: The Animated Adventure (2021)
programme.desc: An Irish boy named Keegan and a Spanish girl named Moya journey into the mythical world of the legendary Megaloceros Giganteus, who teach them to appreciate Riverdance as a celebration of life. Family animation, with the voices of Sam Hardy, Hannah Herman Cortes, Pierce Brosnan and Brendan Gleeson

programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the Galapagos, a collection of volcanic islands in the Pacific known for its extraordinary wildlife. More than 150 years after Charles Darwin formulated his ground-breaking theory of evolution on the remote archipelago, Attenborough explains how life on the islands has continued to evolve in biological isolation, examining how the ever-changing volcanic landscape has given birth to species and sub-species that exist nowhere else in the world. He begins by revealing how Galapagos rose from the ocean four million years ago

programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster reveals how life has learnt to adapt quickly in the ever-changing volcanic landscape, resulting in species that exist nowhere else in the world. David investigates the driving forces behind their evolution, exploring the appearance and behaviour of remarkable creatures and vegetation including giant whale sharks, marine iguanas and seemingly normal dandelion seeds that grow into plants the size of trees

programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster concludes his exploration of the archipelago with a look at how, despite their proximity, no two of the 19 islands are the same. He also uncovers evidence that evolution on Galapagos is occurring faster than Charles Darwin could ever have imagined, and encounters Lonesome George, the last remaining giant Pinta Island tortoise, who died just a few weeks after filming in June 2012

programme.title: Sparkling Cyanide (1983)
programme.desc: An amateur English sleuth on holiday in California becomes involved in the mystery surrounding the poisoning of a lawyer and his promiscuous wife during their anniversary celebrations - and faces the task of solving who among the many guests laced their hosts' champagne. Agatha Christie whodunit, starring Anthony Andrews, Pamela Bellwood, Deborah Raffin, David Huffman and Harry Morgan

programme.desc: The work of officers of the Canada Border Services Agency as its staff enforce customs, quarantine, immigration and finance laws. Whether intercepting suspicious characters, identifying toys packed with drugs or finding weapons disguised as mobile phones, this documentary presents a revealing look at daily life on the front line of national security

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: Another edition of the fly-on-the-wall documentary accessing the world of national security at Australia's biggest airports, following customs and immigration officers as they go about their daily business of tackling drug runners, illegal immigrants and terrorists

programme.title: Deadly Australians
programme.desc: A peek behind the facade of Australia's dramatic desert scenery, where there lurks a motley crew of silent assassins, con-artists, gang members and brutal brawlers

programme.title: Great Parks of Africa
programme.desc: An exploration of the continent's wildlife havens, beginning with a look at Addo Elephant National Park in South Africa

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: The team prepares to release two servals, but things go awry when one vanishes into Malawi's dangerous capital. Plus, a monkey arrives in a critical condition

programme.title: Wildlife ER
programme.desc: Rescue officers Jo and Chantelle free a tangled-up ibis, and vet Mel cares for a baby koala that took a swan dive

programme.title: America's Wild Seasons
programme.desc: As plunging temperatures bring nature into winter, some animals flee south to find milder climes. But others are determined to tough out the brutal cold

programme.title: Kalahari: Land of Secret Alliances
programme.desc: A group of scientists test a hypothesis that animal collaboration is the key to the dizzying diversity of the Kalahari's species

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: Everyone helps with the upbringing of the little lion cubs in Kenya. Plus, a group of bees start a dance party

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: A fish cleans a shark's pearly white teeth, two giraffes have a cuddle and a monkey shows off his toolbox

programme.title: Wild Tales from the Farm
programme.desc: As the leaves change, wild urges grip the animals. Rams Roger and Hamish battle for dominance, while Sally the hen flies the coop for a tour of the farm

programme.title: Wild Tales from the Farm
programme.desc: The farm animals settle down for the shortest day of the year. To stave off boredom, the cows enjoy holiday treats and a concert, while Sheila is busy with her nine piglets

programme.title: Planet Weird
programme.desc: A look at the great white shark's electrical sensors, the razor-sharp limbs of the dead leaf mantis and the zombie-creating powers of cordyceps fungi

programme.title: Planet Weird
programme.desc: The partnership between clownfish and anemones, as well as the quid-pro-poo agreements of dung beetles and pitcher plants

programme.title: Aussie Snake Wranglers: Deadly Pursuit
programme.desc: The deadly world of snake catching Down Under. Breeding season bites as Mick and Adele rescue a Jack Russel from a python. Mick also corners a red-bellied black snake

programme.title: Aussie Snake Wranglers: Deadly Pursuit
programme.desc: Dan and Jess tackle mating eastern browns in a backyard. Mick also finds a venomous snake in couch cushions

programme.title: Qatar: Pearls in the Sand
programme.desc: The desert peninsula of Qatar, exploring its landscape and inhabitants, as well as its transformation from being a centre for pearls, to being known for oil and gas production

programme.title: Baboons: Rules of the Troop
programme.desc: Cameras follow four different species of baboons who have conquered African deserts, coasts, savannah and mountains, adapting to the challenges of their environment along the way

programme.title: Secret Life of the Wombat
programme.desc: A profile of the animal that shares a common ancestor with the koala, but is less well known than its fellow Australian

programme.title: Secret Life of the Wombat
programme.desc: The wombats' mating season, a break from their solitary lives. Despite their timidness, their behaviour here can be surprisingly energetic

programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the Galapagos, a collection of volcanic islands in the Pacific known for its extraordinary wildlife. More than 150 years after Charles Darwin formulated his ground-breaking theory of evolution on the remote archipelago, Attenborough explains how life on the islands has continued to evolve in biological isolation, examining how the ever-changing volcanic landscape has given birth to species and sub-species that exist nowhere else in the world. He begins by revealing how Galapagos rose from the ocean four million years ago

programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster reveals how life has learnt to adapt quickly in the ever-changing volcanic landscape, resulting in species that exist nowhere else in the world. David investigates the driving forces behind their evolution, exploring the appearance and behaviour of remarkable creatures and vegetation including giant whale sharks, marine iguanas and seemingly normal dandelion seeds that grow into plants the size of trees

programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster concludes his exploration of the archipelago with a look at how, despite their proximity, no two of the 19 islands are the same. He also uncovers evidence that evolution on Galapagos is occurring faster than Charles Darwin could ever have imagined, and encounters Lonesome George, the last remaining giant Pinta Island tortoise, who died just a few weeks after filming in June 2012

programme.title: Deadly Australians
programme.desc: A peek behind the facade of Australia's dramatic desert scenery, where there lurks a motley crew of silent assassins, con-artists, gang members and brutal brawlers

programme.title: Big Cat Country
programme.desc: The lionesses from the Hollywood pride are in grave trouble, with intruders occupying their prime hunting grounds and the four dangerous Nomad lions blocking their path home

programme.title: Osprey

programme.title: Ocean Parks

programme.title: Ocean Parks

programme.title: Cub Camp

programme.title: Cub Camp

programme.desc: A day in the life of a Bengal tiger mum as she fights to keep her three young cubs safe in India's unforgiving jungle

programme.title: Into the Wild: India

programme.title: Land of Gremlins
programme.desc: A day in the life of a lemur family living in the rainforest of Madagascar's Mantadia National Park

programme.title: Land of Gremlins

programme.title: Land of Gremlins

programme.desc: Stu and Liv outwit a blue tongue lizard, Jess and Dan free a trapped snake, and Mick and Adele rush a python rescue fast

programme.title: David Attenborough's Rise of Animals
programme.sub-title: From the Seas to the Skies
programme.desc: Part one of two. The naturalist explores the evolution of vertebrates over the past 500 million years, beginning with a look at how remarkable advances allowed primitive fish to make the radical move on to land, and then take to the skies with the advent of flight. He uses fossils of both ancient and living species combined with CGI and film to chart their journey out of the water to populate all corners of the globe

programme.title: David Attenborough's Rise of Animals
programme.desc: The naturalist concludes his two-part exploration of the evolution of vertebrates over the past 500 million years by looking at the history of mammals. He traces their journey from humble origins as tiny nocturnal forest-dwellers to their astonishing explosion in diversity and domination of the world following the death of the dinosaurs

programme.title: Attenborough at 100: Behind the Lens
programme.desc: As David Attenborough turns 100, this intimate film presents new interviews, behind-the-scenes footage and extraordinary clips

programme.title: Alaskan Summer
programme.desc: How summer turns Alaska into a true wildlife paradise, from the bears fishing in its salmon-filled waterways to the millions of migratory birds that flock there

programme.desc: Stu and Liv catch a pregnant red-bellied black snake at a busy resort, while Mick and Adele face a lace monitor lizard

programme.title: Wild Castles
programme.desc: Explore the historic home of an extraordinary family of birds of prey in a look at the wildlife living in the nature reserve surrounding Germany's Heidelberg Castle

programme.title: Impossible Animals

programme.title: Kingdom of the Tide
programme.desc: Documentary taking a look at the intertidal zone, the point at which the ocean meets the land, and the struggle for survival facing the creatures that inhabit this realm. programme.title: Wild Rockies
programme.desc: A look at the lakes, rivers and marshes of the Rockies, which flow through the landscape and feed the creatures that call the mountains home

programme.title: Wild Rockies

Final instalment in the sci-fi comedy trilogy, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Mary Steenburgen and Thomas F Wilson

programme.title: Buildings Beo
programme.desc: Carrie Crowley takes a look at inspiring projects across Ireland, where architectural legacies are being preserved and transformed into vital community spaces. In this episode, Carrie visits the historic town of Castleblayney, Co Monaghan, which takes its name from Blayney Castle, once the focal point of the area. Renamed Hope Castle in the 1850s, the castle's gate lodge fell into disrepair with the passage of time. Carrie follows along as the local townspeople come together to reimagine the space

programme.title: Planda go Pláta
programme.desc: Chef Niall Ó Conchúir heads down to Wicklow on a chilly winter's morning to meet Hilda Crampton on her organic farm in Castleruddery. She has an array of wonderful seasonal produce. Niall settles on some leeks and conjures up a plate of them cooked in cider served on a bed of braised lentils - comfort food for a dark and damp winter evening at its simplest. Meanwhile, back in the kitchen, Rebecca Uí Chonaola gets to grips with some beetroot and puts together a terrine with some home-made tofu cheese. She also makes a crispy strudel with shredded celeriac, which she serves up with a simple green olive sauce

programme.title: Joe Burke - Ceiliúradh
programme.desc: Musicians, friends and family celebrate one of Ireland's most-loved and respected musicians, east Galway's Joe Burke who died in February 2021. Renowned for his wit and humour as well as his mastery of the accordion, Joe's influence on generations of Irish musicians was enormous. Here, contributors including Ann Conroy Burke, Mary Conroy, Frankie Gavin, Brian McGrath, Eileen O'Brien, Máire Ní Chathasaigh, Liz and Yvonne Keane, Jackie Daly, Dermot Byrne and Conchubhar Ó Luasa share music and memories in his honour

This time around, they travel up through the midlands from Limerick to Fermanagh, following the route of the majestic River Shannon, immersing themselves in the rich musical and cultural tapestry of the 'belly' of Ireland. In this episode, the duo leave West Kerry and travel to Limerick, into East Clare and up as far as Tipperary

programme.desc: Julie Fowlis and Muireann NicAmhlaoibh visit Galicia accompanied by acclaimed musicians Angus MacKenzie from Skye and multi-instrumentalist Barry Kerr from Dublin. During their trip to northern Spain they explore the city of Santiago de Compostela and take in the music and culture of the region, performing with renowned Spanish musicians including the influential singer Uxía, world-famous pipers Anxo Lorenzo and Susana Seivane and acclaimed Xabier Díaz and Xosé Liz. Julie performs with the folk-singing group Leilía and both presenters visit the famous Mercado de Abastos

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: ITV's revival of the hit BBC sitcom, starring Linda Robson, Pauline Quirke and Lesley Joseph. Sharon's back in her council flat and Tracey's still in Chigwell with younger son Travis (played by Quirke's real-life son Charlie), but what has become of Dorien? Can anything bring all of the gang back together and will anyone else return to the nest? With Matt Willis of Busted fame

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Now that Sharon has given up work, Tracey is the only one in the house bringing in a wage, and it's not nearly enough to pay the bills, so Dorien kindly offers to dip into her savings to help out. However, when she calls the bank it emerges every penny she had has been spent on legal fees. In desperation, the sisters take their old friend to a car-boot sale and sell her beloved collection of designer clothes, right down to the shoes on her feet - which are snapped up by former TOWIE star Amy Childs. Sitcom, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Birds of a Feather

programme.title: Birds of a Feather

Sitcom, guest starring Clive Mantle (Casualty, Holby City) and Jamie Foreman (EastEnders' Derek Branning)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather

programme.title: Benidorm
programme.desc: Following CEO Crystal's whirlwind visit, a dejected, jobless Joyce tries to relax by the pool, but her morning is ruined when the new manager reinstates Jason Gallagher (former Brookside star Philip Olivier) at the bar. And when Janey and Jason find a letter offering Joyce a new job, they formulate a despicable plan. Elsewhere, the Oracle opens up to Noreen while trapped in a lift with his mum for the day, but who will take advantage of his predicament to make a move on Ionela? programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill are in Fareham, Hampshire, to transform Lisa and Mike's unloved plot into a practical, productive garden that the whole family can enjoy. They have a budget of £3,500 to realise their dreams

programme.title: Garden Rescue

programme.desc: A general invites a motley group of guests to stay at his country estate - and chaos ensues. Silent comedy. starring Ronnie Barker, Michael Hordern and Roger Livesey

programme.title: Heartbreakers (2001)
programme.desc: Mother and daughter Max and Page develop a canny system of conning rich bachelors out of a fortune. Max marries the target, then Page seduces him, allowing for a lucrative divorce settlement. However, the scheme is threatened when Page falls in love, while a former victim tracks Max down hoping to give their marriage another chance. Comedy, starring Sigourney Weaver, Jennifer Love Hewitt, Gene Hackman, Ray Liotta and Jason Lee.

programme.title: Inside Out (1975)
programme.desc: A conman assembles a gang of crooks to retrieve a cache of gold bullion hidden in Germany since the end of the Second World War. However, the only person who knows its location is an imprisoned Nazi war criminal, so they first have to spring him from jail and then return him to his cell once they have the loot - without the authorities knowing. Comedy thriller, with Telly Savalas, James Mason, Robert Culp and Gunter Meisner

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: Mark Evans meets more people who aspire to live in their ultimate waterside dream home. Simon and Mary Sparrow live on a barge in Beccles, Suffolk, with their son Nate and their two cats. The Sparrows have been running a boatyard for nearly 10 years, specialising in refurbishing and renting out floating homes to holidaymakers. Now they're designing and building a new, one-of-a-kind floating home for themselves - a futuristic-looking floating pod, drawing on a budget of £70,000

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: Alan and Val McCoy put their life savings on the line to make their dream of living on water come true, entrusting a boat builder to make them their dream narrow boat floating home. The couple have been together for 11 years and now that they're retired, they've decided that owning a narrow boat could be a unique way to be near their families - all of whom live along the canal system across the Midlands and north-west England

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: This edition charts the exploits of Pat and Sue Willan, who now retired plan to buy their very last floating home, which they hope to keep moored on the Thames. They have enlisted the help of a boat-maker in Stoke and opted for a 35-tonne, 55-foot state-of-the-art, all-mod-cons Dutch barge. They have a healthy budget of over £400,000 and elaborate plans for very contemporary kitchen fittings and gadgets and a powerful hydraulic engine

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: South African-born Linda, her husband Mark and Coco the cat share a one-bedroom floating home on Tagg's Island in Hampton, Surrey. It's an idyllic location, but their home is also their workplace and it's starting to feel a bit cramped. The couple want to stay put in their exclusive location, so Linda has hatched an ambitious plan to buy the boat next door and do it up all by herself. It's a huge challenge and it remains to be seen whether enthusiasm and passion will be enough to get the job done

programme.title: The McKenzie Break (1970)
programme.desc: An Irish prison officer is specially sent to help foil a wily German inmate plotting to escape from a Scottish internment camp - along with several hundred of his countrymen. Wartime thriller, starring Brian Keith, Ian Hendry, Helmut Griem, Patrick O'Connell and Jack Watson

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Lee Burkhill and Chris Hull come to the aid of a couple in Bristol who need a new garden - the only trouble is they have very different ideas about what they want it to be

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Becky and Paul and their two children live on the edge of the picturesque Quantocks near Taunton, Somerset, but are struggling with the strange dog-legged shape of their new-build garden. Charlie Dimmock and Chris Hull use a £6,000 budget to transform its landscaping and introduce a cracked paving patio and bespoke seating area, with a dry stone wall façade. While Chris drives the main garden build, Charlie get to tackle the dog-leg, which Chris has designed to be a fun kids area with a water play area and wooden musical instruments

programme.desc: Tara visits the Comino island and explores the rugged landscapes of Mdina. Later learns about locals in Marsaxlokk and enjoys her time at Popeye Village

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: Touring you to a contrast country of tradition and modernity Bhutan, Paro is an important place to explore the country that truly knows how to preserve its heritage.

programme.title: Backpack Israel
programme.desc: From a mosque built by Ottomans to a city which stood witness to Christ?s miracles, Sabrina finds out about Israel?s ancient roots

programme.title: UK Crime Files: Gangland Murders
programme.desc: Simon Toyne travels to Manchester to examine the gang violence, and turf wars, which have shaped the criminal landscape of one of the UK's most bustling cities.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: Saturday Night Strangler
programme.desc: A look at the case of Joseph Kappen, who murdered three young women in South Wales in 1973.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: Newport Revenge Murder
programme.desc: A look at how an assault investigation jeopardises Robert Henry Davies' new life, and a reign of terror begins in Newport, culminating in a murder.

programme.title: Murder by the Sea
programme.desc: The story of Margaret McOnie, who was found dead on a clifftop. A nationwide manhunt followed, and her killer would soon be revealed as a deadly conman and a double murderer.

programme.title: Murder UK
programme.desc: The story of a betrayal between feuding neighbours in the UK, which was revealed on a confession tape after David Clarke's death.

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: An extreme sportsman reveals the footage he filmed of himself falling down a 1,000ft cliff and discusses how he managed to survive - despite his parachute failing to open properly. Plus, a six-year-old girl takes charge when her mother falls into a coma at the wheel. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A man is filmed while being rescued from the northern Pacific after enduring 90 minutes alone in the cold waters. Plus, a road worker tells the tale of what happens when drivers fail to see traffic cones

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles presents footage of a 70-year-old pilot who is hurled out of his glider after a mid-air collision, and a man sets out to film a skiing race with his father, but ends up filming an accident instead

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A farmer is speared on his own tractor, with the scale of the drama captured on a recording of a neighbour's 999 call for an ambulance. Plus, a solo free climber films his own fall from a sheer rock face. Presented by Nick Knowles

Wendy Craig, Keith Allen and Susie Amy guest star in the island whodunit

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: The inspector and his ambitious sergeant John Bacchus investigate the murder of Dolores Kenny, a black woman. She is a regular attender of a local club's soul all-nighters, events popular with factory workers from across the racial divide. Gently begins to uncover evidence of prejudice within the local community and his own police force as he seeks to identify the victim's killer. Detective drama set in 1968, starring Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby, with Lenora Crichlow, Craig Conway, Pippa Bennett-Warner and Maggie O'Neill

Everything points to suicide - except for the fact that the victim only painted one fingernail. With guest appearances by Denis Lawson, Elizabeth Berrington and Jemima Rooper

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: DI Mooney and the team enter the high-stakes world of poker when Bobby Rodrigues, the favourite to win the Caribbean Masters tournament, is poisoned mid-game. As all fellow players are confined to their seats for the entire match, and no other individual is allowed access to the stage during that time, suspicion soon falls upon each player. A novice to poker, Jack enlists Dwayne's assistance in getting the players to show their hand, while Florence, who is forced to keep away from work after an injury, finds her own way to help the case. Nigel Planer, Mark Benton and Zoe Telford guest star

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Famous thriller author Frank O'Toole is found stabbed through the heart after taking an early morning swim in the ocean. Suspicion soon falls on the victim's wife Valerie, but with eyewitnesses confirming he was swimming alone, how could anyone have got from the house to the sea without being detected? Away from the investigation, Dwayne concentrates on finding a date for a voodoo love festival. Whodunit, guest starring Simon Callow, Imogen Stubbs, and Sian Gibson of Car Share fame

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team investigates when an entrepreneur is found dead in a ravine, but things take a turn when his family's digital marketing guru suddenly disappears. Neville's mum Melanie arrives on the island and teams up with Catherine to take the Saint Marie dating scene by storm and the Commissioner's big Christmas lights switch-on comes to an awkward stop. Caribbean-set detective drama, starring Ralf Little, Don Warrington, Doon Mackichan and Patsy Kensit

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The leader of a spiritual retreat is found strangled en route to the contemplation garden, giving Jack plenty to think about as he tries to figure out which member of the group was responsible. The problem is, they were all meditating at the time of the murder, and are able to provide one another with alibis, so how could any of them be the killer? To add to the DI's problems, he is struggling to come up with a speech to honour Commissioner Patterson at an award ceremony. Caribbean whodunit, starring Ardal O'Hanlon

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: DI Mooney and the team become embroiled in the mysterious world of faith healing when a blind woman dies while being treated by renowned healer Steadman King. When it turns out the victim was poisoned, all eyes turn to Steadman and his cup of holy water. But how could the prime suspect have carried out the deed in front of an audience without anyone noticing? Meanwhile, JP is put through his paces when Pico the police dog joins the team on a week-long trial, and Florence tries to recreate some of Jack's home comforts

She is a regular attender of a local club's soul all-nighters, events popular with factory workers from across the racial divide. Gently begins to uncover evidence of prejudice within the local community and his own police force as he seeks to identify the victim's killer. Detective drama set in 1968, starring Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby, with Lenora Crichlow, Craig Conway, Pippa Bennett-Warner and Maggie O'Neill

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest is stunned when he realises that the fugitive seeking sanctuary at St Mary's is Inspector Sullivan - and the man is wanted for murder. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams, Tom Chambers and Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest investigates the murder of a B-movie director when the production of Demon Brides comes to Kembleford. Crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Sid falls for the heiress to a local brewery, only for her to be arrested for the murder of her own father. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest investigates the poisoning of a former hangman, while romance is in the air for Mrs McCarthy. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Saint-Pierre
programme.desc: When an archaeologist winds up dead in her dig site, Arch and Fitz look into a group of academics she was teaching to find the murderer

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: As the team rushes to save a hostage from an anonymous abductor, they rely on the only witness to her kidnapping: a non-verbal autistic child

programme.title: Allegiance
programme.desc: When an Indigenous woman has her baby taken by a social worker, Sabrina and Vince uncover a web of lies and race against time to find a newborn before she disappears forever

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Detective Inspector Jack Mooney and his team are tested when a billionaire's fiancee falls from a balcony the day before her lavish wedding. Everything points to suicide - except for the fact that the victim only painted one fingernail. With guest appearances by Denis Lawson, Elizabeth Berrington and Jemima Rooper

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: DI Mooney and the team enter the high-stakes world of poker when Bobby Rodrigues, the favourite to win the Caribbean Masters tournament, is poisoned mid-game. As all fellow players are confined to their seats for the entire match, and no other individual is allowed access to the stage during that time, suspicion soon falls upon each player. A novice to poker, Jack enlists Dwayne's assistance in getting the players to show their hand, while Florence, who is forced to keep away from work after an injury, finds her own way to help the case. Nigel Planer, Mark Benton and Zoe Telford guest star

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest is stunned when he realises that the fugitive seeking sanctuary at St Mary's is Inspector Sullivan - and the man is wanted for murder. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams, Tom Chambers and Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: DI Mooney and the team become embroiled in the mysterious world of faith healing when a blind woman dies while being treated by renowned healer Steadman King. When it turns out the victim was poisoned, all eyes turn to Steadman and his cup of holy water. But how could the prime suspect have carried out the deed in front of an audience without anyone noticing? Meanwhile, JP is put through his paces when Pico the police dog joins the team on a week-long trial, and Florence tries to recreate some of Jack's home comforts

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Murdoch and Ogden investigate an inmate's suspicious suicide, reported by the imprisoned former Chief Constable Giles. Crime drama, starring Yannick Bisson and Helene Joy

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team investigates when an entrepreneur is found dead in a ravine, but things take a turn when his family's digital marketing guru suddenly disappears. Neville's mum Melanie arrives on the island and teams up with Catherine to take the Saint Marie dating scene by storm and the Commissioner's big Christmas lights switch-on comes to an awkward stop. Caribbean-set detective drama, starring Ralf Little, Don Warrington, Doon Mackichan and Patsy Kensit

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Famous thriller author Frank O'Toole is found stabbed through the heart after taking an early morning swim in the ocean. Suspicion soon falls on the victim's wife Valerie, but with eyewitnesses confirming he was swimming alone, how could anyone have got from the house to the sea without being detected? Away from the investigation, Dwayne concentrates on finding a date for a voodoo love festival. Whodunit, guest starring Simon Callow, Imogen Stubbs, and Sian Gibson of Car Share fame

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Another mystery for Ardal O'Hanlon as Irish detective Jack Mooney working on the Caribbean island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. And so it appears this week as someone from JP's past is caught red-handed at a murder scene, raking up old memories for the young officer. But can the DI unravel the complex mystery to get at the truth? Tobi Bakare and Danny John-Jules co-star, with guest appearances from Ralph Brown and Adjoa Andoh

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond travel to Africa, where they are challenged to buy a second-hand car costing less than £1,500 and in no way designed to go off-road, and drive across Botswana. Along the way they must negotiate salt pans, rivers and reserves full of animals while facing sweltering heat and suffocating dust

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Festive edition from 2008 in which Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on one of the most spectacular road trips they have ever attempted. Arriving in Vietnam, they are challenged to travel the entire length of the country in just eight days. First they must buy suitable transport for the journey, and despite having several million Dong in their pockets, this proves more difficult than they expected, especially in one of the most expensive car markets in the world

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse borrows a vintage Porsche from an old pal, so he and Bob Mortimer travel in style to the Avon in Hampshire, where they are fishing for roach, dace and river carp - a species they have yet to target. During the trip, Bob Mortimer makes the most of their treehouse accommodation and Ted has a blind date with a Patterdale called Effie - so the duo give the terrier a pep talk on the does and don'ts of doggie dating

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer venture to Sutton Lake, a sequestered lake in Shropshire, where they have their eyes set on catching a crucian carp. This is the duo's second attempt to net the elusive fish and they are changing tactics - staying overnight in bivvies in the hope of guaranteeing success. Away from the angling, the duo reminisce about vintage music and old relatives and find time for a trip to the pub

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: The friends head to the tidal outcrop of Burgh Island on the south coast of Devon to fish for wrasse, and Bob kickstarts their trip with a huge surprise for Paul. Keen to test the surrounding waters, Paul walks down to the beach, but is met with an unexpected but familiar face, while he also encounters a rather unconventional fishing approach. Will he be tempted to stray from his tried and tested methods?

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: TV presenters Alex Jones and Rick Edwards, comedian Greg Davies and performance poet John Cooper Clarke join forces with team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the panel show in which participants attempt to deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack return with previously unseen material from the ninth series of the comedy panel show. programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon introduces a seasonal special of the comedy panel game. Musical comedian Bill Bailey, actor and writer Ruth Jones, comedian and presenter Jo Brand and athlete-turned-presenter Kelly Holmes join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack, trying to hoodwink their opponents with a few festive fibs or facts about themselves

programme.title: Red Dwarf

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy take the children out trick or treating for Halloween, but the night soon turns disastrous for Lee when he loses his mobile on the porch of a spooky Victorian house. After returning to the property to retrieve his phone, Lee discovers that the building is full of secrets that would be far better left undisturbed

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.sub-title: Not Going Out Christmas Special: Driving Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy pop out on a last-minute Christmas Eve mission, leaving their youngsters in the apparently capable hands of Geoffrey - cutting more of a strict and stuffy Victorian gent than usual - and wife Wendy. Alas, things don't quite go to plan for the outward-bound duo, as a theft and unexpected trip into deep countryside await. Not only that, but there are encounters with an aggressive bull in a field and a terrifying truck driver to endure. Meanwhile, back at home, Lee's lovably feckless father Frank turns up to offer gifts - in his own inimitable way. Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Bobby Ball star

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.sub-title: Sarah Millican, Gary Delaney, Laura Lexx, Ahir Shah
programme.desc: Sarah Millican hosts the fourth annual Christmas special, recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. Taking to the stage for tonight's festive line-up are Ahir Shah and Laura Lexx, two performers making their Live at the Apollo debuts following successful shows at this year's Edinburgh Fringe, while the bill is completed by Gary Delaney

programme.title: Mel Giedroyc: Unforgivable
programme.desc: Mel and Lou are joined by Ross Kemp, Shirley Ballas and Rosie Jones as Shirley divulges her revenge on a cheating husband and Ross spills all on a prison-based prank

programme.title: Mel Giedroyc: Unforgivable
programme.desc: Mel and Lou Sanders are joined by Daisy May Cooper, Ardal O'Hanlon and Emmanuel Sonubi as Emmanuel explains why his towels are sticky and Daisy gets creative

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: April and Andy host a dinner party for the Parks Department, and Ann attempts to move on from Chris by attending a singles' event. Comedy, starring Amy Poehler, Aubrey Plaza and Rashida Jones

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Special in which Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May take three used, V8-engined sports cars on a gruelling road trip covering 1,600 miles through Patagonia to the world's southernmost city. Having encountered stunning landscapes, mechanical mishaps and dreadful roads on their journey through Argentina, the trio's adventure is brought to a dramatic climax by a terrifying turn of events

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob and Paul return to the Peak District, this time with Ted, where they filmed their first episode of Gone Fishing six years ago. They start by fishing in the Wye at Haddon Hall, which was built in the 11th century and has been visited by countless kings and queens, with Paul on a quest to catch a rainbow trout. They then head off to the Derwent, where they compare their and Ted's health to that of the UK's rivers

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer, Paul Whitehouse and trusty canine companion Ted travel to Scotland to fish for salmon in the Dee, Tay and Ericht, and to taste the traditional Hogmanay celebrations on offer. They start their trip on a steam train overlooking the Dee, the first place they'll fish, a river that brings back special memories for Paul as he used to fish the same beat with his father for many years. Mystic Wilf also drops by to predict their year ahead, and Ted is treated to a special afternoon's entertainment before the traditional works party and awards ceremony. Their final guest arrives to give them a very special performance. Clare Grogan and her band Altered Images play as fireworks rain over their party and they all bid farewell to 2023 and welcome 2024

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers: Mums Know Best
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers track down traditional, yet flamboyant, recipes usually reserved for special occasions. They sample a Baltic take on a pig's trotters dish, try a lemon souffle said to be favoured by the royal family, and learn the secrets behind the perfect baked Alaska. The duo also demonstrate how to prepare a prized honey-glazed ham

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers: Mums Know Best
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers examine a variety of traditional dishes reserved for birthdays, learning how to make Manchester tart, Greek stuffed vine leaves and a Caribbean Saturday soup. They also demonstrate how to prepare a summer berry trifle, and invite 200 women to sample one another's specialities

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary in which the presenter takes a tour of the country, travelling from the islands of the Aegean to Athens. Along the way he meets local people including gun-toting priests and the last sponge-divers in the world, and discovers how they are coping with their country's current crises

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. Simon travels from the Peloponnese peninsula into the mountainous north of the country. He meets local people including a group of rebel monks and conservationists caring for an injured bear, and discovers how they are coping with the political crises affecting Greece

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Documentary with unrestricted access to America's Customs and Border Protection working on the bridges that separate Texas and Mexico

programme.title: Special Ops: Crime Squad UK
programme.desc: Surrey police investigate drug dealers, farmers in Northamptonshire are targeted by a gang in the countryside and a crime squad in Kent investigate local burglaries

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You

programme.title: QI

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Maisie Adam, Ed Byrne, Larry Dean, Ed Gamble, Tiff Stevenson and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: Coverage of the offbeat event, where teams create vehicles without engines and race downhill

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond travel to Africa, where they are challenged to buy a second-hand car costing less than £1,500 and in no way designed to go off-road, and drive across Botswana. Along the way they must negotiate salt pans, rivers and reserves full of animals while facing sweltering heat and suffocating dust

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Festive edition from 2008 in which Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on one of the most spectacular road trips they have ever attempted. Arriving in Vietnam, they are challenged to travel the entire length of the country in just eight days. First they must buy suitable transport for the journey, and despite having several million Dong in their pockets, this proves more difficult than they expected, especially in one of the most expensive car markets in the world

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse borrows a vintage Porsche from an old pal, so he and Bob Mortimer travel in style to the Avon in Hampshire, where they are fishing for roach, dace and river carp - a species they have yet to target. During the trip, Bob Mortimer makes the most of their treehouse accommodation and Ted has a blind date with a Patterdale called Effie - so the duo give the terrier a pep talk on the does and don'ts of doggie dating

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer venture to Sutton Lake, a sequestered lake in Shropshire, where they have their eyes set on catching a crucian carp. This is the duo's second attempt to net the elusive fish and they are changing tactics - staying overnight in bivvies in the hope of guaranteeing success. Away from the angling, the duo reminisce about vintage music and old relatives and find time for a trip to the pub

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: The friends head to the tidal outcrop of Burgh Island on the south coast of Devon to fish for wrasse, and Bob kickstarts their trip with a huge surprise for Paul. Keen to test the surrounding waters, Paul walks down to the beach, but is met with an unexpected but familiar face, while he also encounters a rather unconventional fishing approach. Will he be tempted to stray from his tried and tested methods?

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: TV presenters Alex Jones and Rick Edwards, comedian Greg Davies and performance poet John Cooper Clarke join forces with team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the panel show in which participants attempt to deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: The crew members emerge from suspended animation to find they have lost Red Dwarf, and their search for the ship takes them into a region infested with Psirens - telepathic predators who take the form of their victims' greatest desires. programme.title: Red Dwarf

programme.title: Top Gear

Having encountered stunning landscapes, mechanical mishaps and dreadful roads on their journey through Argentina, the trio's adventure is brought to a dramatic climax by a terrifying turn of events

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob and Paul return to the Peak District, this time with Ted, where they filmed their first episode of Gone Fishing six years ago. They start by fishing in the Wye at Haddon Hall, which was built in the 11th century and has been visited by countless kings and queens, with Paul on a quest to catch a rainbow trout. They then head off to the Derwent, where they compare their and Ted's health to that of the UK's rivers

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer, Paul Whitehouse and trusty canine companion Ted travel to Scotland to fish for salmon in the Dee, Tay and Ericht, and to taste the traditional Hogmanay celebrations on offer. They start their trip on a steam train overlooking the Dee, the first place they'll fish, a river that brings back special memories for Paul as he used to fish the same beat with his father for many years. Mystic Wilf also drops by to predict their year ahead, and Ted is treated to a special afternoon's entertainment before the traditional works party and awards ceremony. Their final guest arrives to give them a very special performance. Clare Grogan and her band Altered Images play as fireworks rain over their party and they all bid farewell to 2023 and welcome 2024

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers: Mums Know Best
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers track down traditional, yet flamboyant, recipes usually reserved for special occasions. They sample a Baltic take on a pig's trotters dish, try a lemon souffle said to be favoured by the royal family, and learn the secrets behind the perfect baked Alaska. The duo also demonstrate how to prepare a prized honey-glazed ham

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers: Mums Know Best
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers examine a variety of traditional dishes reserved for birthdays, learning how to make Manchester tart, Greek stuffed vine leaves and a Caribbean Saturday soup. They also demonstrate how to prepare a summer berry trifle, and invite 200 women to sample one another's specialities

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary in which the presenter takes a tour of the country, travelling from the islands of the Aegean to Athens. programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. Simon travels from the Peloponnese peninsula into the mountainous north of the country. He meets local people including a group of rebel monks and conservationists caring for an injured bear, and discovers how they are coping with the political crises affecting Greece

Drama, starring Ian McShane, Dudley Sutton, Caroline Langrishe and Susan Fleetwood

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Lovejoy finds an unusual Ming Dynasty war cannon being used as a plant pot. His client asks him to get the best price for the item - but the Chinese community objects to any such proposed deal in no uncertain terms and when Charlotte takes the dodgy dealer to London to sell it they are abducted. Burt Kwouk guest stars, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: When faced with a hefty dentist bill, Lovejoy agrees to settle it by investigating the alleged death by drowning of Terence Sullivan, a fellow antiques dealer. It doesn't take long to determine that Sullivan is still alive, having taken up residence with an attractive stewardess. When Charlotte sees Lovejoy's caring side towards the 'widow', she feels she may have misjudged his character and finally gives in to his seductive charm. Pete Postlethwaite guest stars, with Ian McShane

programme.desc: Tensions rise to deadly levels when a performance troupe of anarchists arrive in Kembleford, leaving Mrs McCarthy unimpressed and Bunty inspired

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra and the team unearth the 10-year-old case of a poet from Belfast whose burnt body was found in the scrapyard of a known gangster. Gerry becomes convinced the victim's links with the criminal fraternity led to his demise, but Brian has a different theory, believing the secret of his murder lies in his verse. Amanda Redman, Dennis Waterman and Alun Armstrong star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the murder of an East German immigrant, but all they have to go on are his mysterious final words, which translate as 'blue flower'. As they piece together his remarkable story, Sandra tries to gain the trust of his estranged daughter. Crime drama, starring Amanda Redman

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A man arrives in an East Anglian village with his sister Joanna to convalesce following a flying accident - but the residents' warm welcome isn't all it seems. Joan Hickson stars as the elderly sleuth, with Andrew Bicknell and Sabina Franklyn

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: When popular local radio host Ryan Osbourne is killed in an explosion at his old high school, the shockwaves rattle the community as a fundraising initiative ends in tragedy. The hidden rivalries and secrets that emerge create a long list of suspects for Shepherd. Drama, starring Neill Rea

programme.title: Monsieur Spade
programme.desc: Spade searches for answers regarding the mysterious young boy everyone seems to be looking for. Samir takes him into hiding, but no one knows what to make of his incessant writing

programme.title: The Last Detective
programme.desc: Excessive flushing sounds convince an elderly woman that her neighbour has killed his wife and is disposing of her body parts via the toilet. Naturally, Davies is sent to investigate, and it isn't long before the detective has gained a broken nose and an obsessive stalker. Meanwhile, Mod lands a job in the mobile catering industry. Anna Wing (once of EastEnders) guest stars in the detective drama, with Peter Davison and Sean Hughes

programme.desc: Great Slaughter's twinning with a German town is met with animosity by some members of the twinning committee and townsfolk. Karl Fischer, the German mayor, with his wife Elsa and a group of German soldiers led by General Udo Von Stark arrive for the ceremony. An attempt on Fischer's life kills Ellis Everett, who was opposed to the twinning. Sister Boniface investigates whether it's an accident or a malicious plan and soon discovers a handkerchief soaked with sweat and smelling of acetone. Mystery drama, starring Lorna Watson

programme.desc: Drama about the lives of a group of Women's Institute members in a rural Cheshire community during the Second World War. In the face of an impending conflict with Germany, the Great Paxford WI faces a difficult decision. When president Joyce Cameron resigns, taking most of the membership with her, Frances Barden and her supporters must recruit new helpers from unexpected places. Starring Samantha Bond, Francesca Annis, Ruth Gemmell, Claire Rushbrook and Frances Grey, with Ed Stoppard and Mark Bazeley

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: In 1930, recovering from a personal crisis, Agatha Christie fell in love with and married Max Mallowan. This relationship, and the security it brought, saw Agatha enter the most prolific and successful chapter of her career. This would be the period in which Agatha penned some of her most famous and enduring bestsellers, including Murder on the Orient Express.
