programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales return to the Oxford Canal, the waterway where they first fell in love with boating more than 40 years ago. Aboard their own boat, they travel north from Banbury, heading for the old canal port of Braunston, joined by their daughter Juliet and sons Sam and Joe. As they navigate their way through the beautiful Cherwell Valley, the couple look back at some their favourite voyages on the inland waterways of Britain and Ireland. As well as remembering great moments of joy and wonder, the voyage is tinged with sadness given the recent deterioration in Pru's dementia

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Veteran actors and married couple Timothy West and Prunella Scales celebrate their 50th wedding anniversary with four adventures around the canal networks of Britain and France. In the first edition, the pair head to the West Country to travel along the fully restored Kennet and Avon canal. Taking in the sights, characters and wildlife from Bath to Devizes, Wiltshire, the duo enjoy an eventful journey along the picturesque 21-mile stretch of water

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Veteran actors and married couple Timothy West and Prunella Scales continue their journey and reach the Rochdale Canal - a waterway that was a main artery for the textile industry at the height of the Industrial Revolution. They begin in Sowerby Bridge, West Yorkshire, and head west through Mytholmroyd, before journeying up over the Pennines to the canal summit, more than 600ft above sea level

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Rodney and Mickey Pearce are commissioned to make a community film, but Del-Boy soon gets in on the act, offering a role to anyone who wants one - provided they pay for the privilege

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee imagines having the perfect life with Lucy, but the reality may not be good enough to match up to his dreams. Comedy, guest starring Bobby Ball, with Lee Mack, Tim Vine, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: First episode of the sitcom about a drifter whose friendship with his flatmate is complicated by the fact her ex-boyfriend is his best mate. Lee dates an author in an effort to discover his serious side, Kate tries to lighten up at clown school and Tim finally apologises for his past behaviour. Lee Mack, Megan Dodds and Tim Vine star

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes is disappointed to discover she is not invited to Maria's hen party because of her age, and decides to gatecrash the event, only to end up at the wrong venue. She then tries to impress the bride-to-be's mother during a dinner date, but is so nervous she turns to drink, and the evening descends into chaos. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: Don't Wait Up
programme.desc: Toby makes use of a deputising service to enjoy a night off. Tom has problems with the landlord of his surgery - his ex-wife Helen

programme.title: Alma's Not Normal
programme.desc: When Alma discovers that her care-leaving background can help secure her an audition to join a theatre company for a national tour, she jumps at the chance. However, when social services sends through her records, Alma comes face to face with the stark reality of her childhood growing up in and out of the care system. Could this bombshell lead to Alma missing out on an opportunity she's been waiting her whole life for? Comedy, written by and starring Sophie Willan

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie and Tyler are united in their distaste when Marlene demands that the family pays a visit to her mother in Bournemouth. Elgin, Jed, and Mrs Cakeworthy take desperate measures when Bryan begins to mope as the anniversary of his fourth engagement to Myrtle draws ever closer. Guest starring June Whitfield

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.sub-title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: An old friend returns from Australia flushed with success and offers Del a share in his business empire Down Under. Although the prospect is tempting, it would mean leaving Nelson Mandela House and younger brother Rodney behind. David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst star

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del and Rodney reluctantly agree to let Grandad accompany them on their holiday abroad - a decision they come to regret. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Lennard Pearce

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Rodney and Mickey Pearce are commissioned to make a community film, but Del-Boy soon gets in on the act, offering a role to anyone who wants one - provided they pay for the privilege

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del jumps at the opportunity to decorate the local Chinese restaurant to make a quick profit, and the results are outstanding in more ways than one. Comedy, with David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Lennard Pearce

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: The Trotters agree to dog-sit while Boycie and Marlene go on holiday, and happily tuck into the fresh meat lovingly provided for their charge, scarcely suspecting what lies ahead. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Tempers flare between the Trotter brothers as they fall for the same woman - and Del's efforts to win the romance war leave his future hanging in the balance. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Geraldine's hopes of romance are fuelled by a visit from David's handsome brother, while the Hortons return from honeymoon with a souvenir they had not bargained for - Alice is pregnant. The only problem is, she doesn't know it yet. Clerical comedy, starring Dawn French and Emma Chambers. From Christmas Eve 1999

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Alice suggests an unconventional nativity play starring herself and Hugo as Mary and Joseph, and adds an extra touch of authenticity by going into labour mid-performance. The coincidence is so striking that she asks Geraldine whether she has given birth to the Son of God - even though her baby is a girl. Festive episode from 1999, starring Dawn French and Emma Chambers

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Alice and Hugo's baby is christened. The godparents are chosen, the Bishop of Mulberry officiates, and David asks Geraldine to marry him. She consents to his proposal - until sense prevails. Comedy, guest starring Sean Bean, with Dawn French, Gary Waldhorn, James Fleet and Emma Chambers

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: A summer water shortage presents problems for the locals, especially Alice, whose lack of clean clothes forces her to wear a Wonder Woman outfit instead. The parched residents soon see events turned on their head as the water company offers to resolve the problem by flooding the village to create a reservoir

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Geraldine's hopes of romance are fuelled by a visit from David's handsome brother, while the Hortons return from honeymoon with a souvenir they had not bargained for - Alice is pregnant. The only problem is, she doesn't know it yet. Clerical comedy, starring Dawn French and Emma Chambers. From Christmas Eve 1999

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Alice suggests an unconventional nativity play starring herself and Hugo as Mary and Joseph, and adds an extra touch of authenticity by going into labour mid-performance. The coincidence is so striking that she asks Geraldine whether she has given birth to the Son of God - even though her baby is a girl. Festive episode from 1999, starring Dawn French and Emma Chambers

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Alice and Hugo's baby is christened. The godparents are chosen, the Bishop of Mulberry officiates, and David asks Geraldine to marry him. She consents to his proposal - until sense prevails. Comedy, guest starring Sean Bean, with Dawn French, Gary Waldhorn, James Fleet and Emma Chambers

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: A summer water shortage presents problems for the locals, especially Alice, whose lack of clean clothes forces her to wear a Wonder Woman outfit instead. The parched residents soon see events turned on their head as the water company offers to resolve the problem by flooding the village to create a reservoir

programme.title: Open All Hours: Inside Out
programme.desc: Diane Morgan narrates this special documentary about the much-loved comedy, featuring contributions from Sir David Jason, Johnny Vegas, Tim Healy, Ronnie Barker's daughter Charlotte, and more

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Tempers flare between the Trotter brothers as they fall for the same woman - and Del's efforts to win the romance war leave his future hanging in the balance. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del and Rodney reluctantly agree to let Grandad accompany them on their holiday abroad - a decision they come to regret. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Lennard Pearce

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.sub-title: The Long Legs of the Law
programme.desc: Rodney starts going out with a policewoman, much to the consternation of dodgy dealer Del-Boy, who is terrified that one wrong word from his little brother could see him banged up for five years. Vintage comedy, starring Nicholas Lyndhurst, David Jason and Lennard Pearce

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del jumps at the opportunity to decorate the local Chinese restaurant to make a quick profit, and the results are outstanding in more ways than one. Comedy, with David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Lennard Pearce

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses

programme.title: Alma's Not Normal
programme.desc: When Alma discovers that her care-leaving background can help secure her an audition to join a theatre company for a national tour, she jumps at the chance. However, when social services sends through her records, Alma comes face to face with the stark reality of her childhood growing up in and out of the care system. Could this bombshell lead to Alma missing out on an opportunity she's been waiting her whole life for? Comedy, written by and starring Sophie Willan

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mike and Alison welcome the very first guests to their new bijoux B&B. While serving the couple breakfast, Alison realises that they are not enjoying their break and, fearing a bad review, utilises the surprising skills of Lady Button and Julian to ensure the pair make the most of their weekend away. Thomas is perplexed yet secretly thrilled by the sudden attention from the Plaguers, who, after admiring Alison's discarded portrait of him are now affording him celebrity status in Button House, hanging on to his every word. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Alma's Not Normal
programme.desc: Things are not going well between Lin and Joan and the news that Alma will be leaving for a six-month tour looks like it might push Lin over the edge. When the social worker visits, Lin snaps and disappears, leaving Alma fearing that her mum may have turned back to her old ways

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mary walks Julian through her journey of self-discovery and how she finally found her voice. Thomas realises enough is enough - he must go 'cold turkey' on his Alison addiction and needs Pat's undivided attention. Old friends of Mike check in to the guesthouse and agree to share the surprising secret of their success. The Captain is thrown by a change to his daily routine, so Kitty steps in to teach him how to live in the moment. Robin helps Lady Button to remember lost friends

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary looks for a new flatmate following Dermot's departure, and along comes Tony - but he may not be the perfect choice he appears to be. Meanwhile, Anthea's requests for a pay rise are greeted with banishment to the stationery cupboard

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: First episode of Richard Curtis's comedy, starring Dawn French as the new vicar of a sleepy English village. Her arrival causes consternation among the locals - particularly stuffy councillor David Horton, who vows to have her ejected at all costs. Gary Waldhorn, James Fleet and Emma Chambers co-star

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: The Songs of Praise crew visits Dibley to record a programme in the village church. However, when Geraldine develops a crush on the producer her wayward emotions threaten to plunge the proceedings into disaster. Comedy, guest starring Peter Capaldi (The Thick of It)

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Geraldine is keen to find a celebrity to open the autumn fair in style - and Alice inadvertently comes up with the ideal candidate by letting slip she is related to one Reg Dwight - AKA Elton John. Comedy, starring Dawn French, and Emma Chambers with a guest appearance by Kylie Minogue. First shown in 1994

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: A violent storm destroys St Barnabas's stained glass window, and Geraldine is put in charge of raising funds to replace it - but the others are unsure whether any of her outlandish ideas will actually work. Comedy, starring Dawn French, Gary Waldhorn, James Fleet, Emma Chambers and Liz Smith

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Local election fever hits the village and Geraldine finds she is a more popular candidate with the voters than over-confident parish council chairman David. Richard Curtis's comedy, starring Dawn French and Gary Waldhorn

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: A funeral for a pet bird inspires Geraldine to transform the church into an ark and hold a special service for the animals of the village - but David is far from impressed by her stunt. Comedy, starring Dawn French and Gary Waldhorn

programme.desc: Geraldine is keen to find a celebrity to open the autumn fair in style - and Alice inadvertently comes up with the ideal candidate by letting slip she is related to one Reg Dwight - AKA Elton John. Comedy, starring Dawn French, and Emma Chambers with a guest appearance by Kylie Minogue. First shown in 1994

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: A violent storm destroys St Barnabas's stained glass window, and Geraldine is put in charge of raising funds to replace it - but the others are unsure whether any of her outlandish ideas will actually work. Comedy, starring Dawn French, Gary Waldhorn, James Fleet, Emma Chambers and Liz Smith

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Local election fever hits the village and Geraldine finds she is a more popular candidate with the voters than over-confident parish council chairman David. Richard Curtis's comedy, starring Dawn French and Gary Waldhorn

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: A funeral for a pet bird inspires Geraldine to transform the church into an ark and hold a special service for the animals of the village - but David is far from impressed by her stunt. Comedy, starring Dawn French and Gary Waldhorn

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: First episode of Richard Curtis's comedy, starring Dawn French as the new vicar of a sleepy English village. Her arrival causes consternation among the locals - particularly stuffy councillor David Horton, who vows to have her ejected at all costs. Gary Waldhorn, James Fleet and Emma Chambers co-star

programme.title: Open All Hours: Inside Out
programme.desc: Diane Morgan narrates this special documentary about the much-loved comedy, featuring contributions from Sir David Jason, Johnny Vegas, Tim Healy, Ronnie Barker's daughter Charlotte, and more

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: A squeak on Granville's bike interferes with his hopes of romance, and Arkwright worries about a mysterious visitor to a house in Atkinson Terrace

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright is reluctant to buy a new suit until Nurse Gladys Emmanuel persuades him to. He agrees, but only if I can be made in the same style as his old one. Roy Clarke's classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker, David Jason and Lynda Baron

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville encourages Gastric to become a fearless hunter in an effort to impress Madge and get closer to her. Mavis senses Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, making a move on Granville and decides to stand up for herself for once. Mr Newbold goes to the library in the hope of meeting up with Mrs Rossi, while Eric and Cyril finally get their wives to go camping - but who is going to help with the washing-up? David Jason, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville struggles to find some privacy when the milkwoman makes a pass at him, so nurse Gladys Emmanuel steps in by persuading Arkwright to take her to a hotel. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason Granville First broadcast in March 1981

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley

programme.sub-title: The Long Legs of the Law
programme.desc: Rodney starts going out with a policewoman, much to the consternation of dodgy dealer Del-Boy, who is terrified that one wrong word from his little brother could see him banged up for five years. Vintage comedy, starring Nicholas Lyndhurst, David Jason and Lennard Pearce

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.sub-title: Three Men, a Woman and a Baby
programme.desc: Rodney and Albert begin to worry the flat will get overcrowded as the birth of Del-Boy Jr approaches, while Del feels well prepared for fatherhood thanks to a stint at antenatal classes with Raquel. Starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mike and Alison welcome the very first guests to their new bijoux B&B. While serving the couple breakfast, Alison realises that they are not enjoying their break and, fearing a bad review, utilises the surprising skills of Lady Button and Julian to ensure the pair make the most of their weekend away. Thomas is perplexed yet secretly thrilled by the sudden attention from the Plaguers, who, after admiring Alison's discarded portrait of him are now affording him celebrity status in Button House, hanging on to his every word. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mary walks Julian through her journey of self-discovery and how she finally found her voice. Thomas realises enough is enough - he must go 'cold turkey' on his Alison addiction and needs Pat's undivided attention. Old friends of Mike check in to the guesthouse and agree to share the surprising secret of their success. The Captain is thrown by a change to his daily routine, so Kitty steps in to teach him how to live in the moment. Robin helps Lady Button to remember lost friends

programme.title: The Outlaws
programme.desc: Gabby and Greg go cap-in-hand to a possible financier for the festival - her own father. Meanwhile, Diane reveals a murky past, and attempting to lay her own to rest, Myrna reaches out to family. John recruits Frank to help him impress his father, Tom discovers something which threatens his grandad's newfound domestic bliss, and Rani tries to recruit some help for an audacious break-in. Richard E Grant guest stars

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary thinks Tony might be gay, and tries to uncover the truth without asking directly. However, his new flatmate attempts to impress Deborah with his language skills - despite not having any

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: After lending assistance to a wealthy woman in distress, Del seizes a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to improve his social standing by launching his own chandelier-cleaning business. programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A luxury hotel chain makes penniless and exhausted couple Mike and Alison an offer they can't refuse, but the prospect of their departure drives a wedge between the ghosts, and the hardline Captain finds himself ostracised, while Pat is voted in as reluctant leader. As the clueless spirits brainstorm money-making schemes, the Captain takes matters into his own hands. Meanwhile, fortune smiles on Headless Humphrey in his ceaseless search for his errant body

programme.title: Deadly Waters with Captain Lee
programme.desc: After seasickness and choppy waters cause a wife to fall from her husband's fishing vessel, the race is on to save her. However, a deep dive reveals a shocking truth

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: Doug's fiancee won't move in until he clears his tiki collection and his dead ex-wife's things, so the Death Cleaners help him sort out the clutter in his heart and home

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Anna and Darren treat a woman suffering from terminal cancer. Laura and Kerry are called to attend a young man with a painful abscess in his throat

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Key witness Fran Morris (Kelly Harrison) is left with serious head injuries following an attack, and her 12-year-old son goes missing at the same time. Max suspects the drug dealer she helped to catch is behind the assault, but when the boy is finally found he remains tight-lipped and fails to confirm the story. As the case stalls, it is left to forensics to unravel what really happened

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A distressed 17-year-old is found traumatised by years of prolonged abuse and Stevie struggles to establish his identity after the lad's sister fails to recognise him. Max re-examines the evidence from the disappearance of a 12-year-old boy five years previously, hoping to make a connection with someone called Gareth, but it soon becomes clear the troubled young man at the station is not telling the whole story

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When an eight-year-old is abducted from outside his school, Terry is appointed Family Liaison Officer and finds himself keeping the peace between the boy's divorcing parents. CCTV footage reveals the child got into a car with a man after taking a phone call, and the race is on to discover the identities of the mystery caller and the driver. Emma Cunniffe guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Leon's prison officer friend is reluctantly caught up in a plot to smuggle a telephone into Longmarsh jail for a drug dealer. The constable persuades her to go ahead with the plan - despite the risks to herself and her young son - and deliver a bugged phone so CID can continue their surveillance of the gangster. It soon becomes clear the villain has murder in mind. Gemma Atkinson guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A frantic mother reports her car has been stolen with her baby still strapped inside. The suspect is soon traced, but the child is not recovered until later when her father, following an alleged tip-off, traces her to an alleyway where she is found distressed and badly bruised, still strapped in her car seat. Leon is not convinced the injuries are a result of her recent adventure, and suspects the infant has been abused

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Smithy holds a public meeting, hoping to gain the trust and cooperation of hostile residents on the Manley Road estate, who are being terrorised by drug dealers. A plucky shopkeeper takes matters into her own hands to halt the reign of fear, only to find her daughter suffers a backlash from the gang. At the end of a tiring day, Tony Stamp stuns the inspector with unexpected news

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Leon responds to a call from a distraught mother whose 18-month-old child has sustained serious head injuries. She claims the toddler fell from her highchair, but suspicion mounts that the mother's partner may have contravened his restraining order to visit the family. Tony Stamp spends his last shift with Sun Hill comforting the woman, and is shocked when he has to arrest her for harming her own daughter

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A hitman linked to a drugs gang guns down two men in a supermarket car park. Later, a teenage dare leads to a massive rescue operation after a boy vanishes in the water

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A truck drives through the barrier of a busy motorway and hits a BMW head-on. The team join a delicate rescue operation to persuade a suicidal man down from a bridge

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: It's a race against time to dig out a man buried under three tons of soil, and the team are called to rescue the victims of a major crash involving a taxi

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team are called to a major fire in a chemicals factory and paramedics join firefighters desperately searching for teenagers reported missing in a swollen river

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team are called in to save the survivors of a fatal crash and there's a bomb alert after Afghan asylum seekers are moved into a seaside hotel

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A car driven by teenage joyriders plunges into a deep ditch and a punter falls through the floor of his local betting shop in a bizarre accident

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: A boater jumps through a fireball after a gas explosion, a hunter has a heart attack, and fishermen get some much-needed advice

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: A mother and her unborn baby are at risk, adventure biking in the Marlborough Sounds causes a nasty injury, and a Land SAR team saves the day

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Fortune smiles on Mike and Alison when a TV company comes calling, looking for an old house in which to film a period drama. But to get the money in the bank, Alison must ensure the shoot goes without a hitch despite the horde of curious ghosts, the house's crumbling floorboards and her crippling crush on the show's lead actor. Meanwhile, Thomas and Lady Button learn the meaning of a free pass, and Julian tries his luck at betting on horse racing. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Learning that their posh neighbours the Beg-Chetwyndes have an ancient claim over their land, Mike and Alison throw an upmarket dinner party to help resolve a thorny issue. But with the ghosts her only guide to high-class decorum, the evening starts badly and only gets worse - thanks to Mary's woeful catering, Mike's clueless deal-making and Robin's raucous celebration of the lunar eclipse. Elsewhere, the spooks become obsessed with a TV sitcom. Comedy, guest starring Geoff McGivern and Sophie Thompson

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: A death row wife finds love with a convicted murderer, and new parents are determined not to let their baby run their lives. Peter Kay guest stars

programme.title: Who Killed Jenni Rivera?
programme.desc: In 2012, Latin music superstar Jenni Rivera was killed in a plane crash in Mexico - but was it an accident? Speculations about the crash continue today

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: A death row wife finds love with a convicted murderer, and new parents are determined not to let their baby run their lives. Peter Kay guest stars

programme.title: Deadly Waters with Captain Lee
programme.desc: After a mysterious 911 call leads police to a riverboat engulfed in flames, they discover the call may have come from inside the vessel

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: The Death Cleaners help the wife of an American football fan who has died but asked for help cleaning his 'man cave' before his passing

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Janine and Adam assist a woman who is threatening to jump out of a first-floor window, and husband and wife team Ann and Kevin are called out to help Ann's sick Dad

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: The arrival of French exchange student Sylvie makes for a decidedly unhappy family. Nick appoints himself a one-man welcoming committee and offers to put her up in his room, but Janey harbours ill feelings toward her, dashing hopes of improving Anglo-French relations

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben and Susan look forward to their well-deserved holiday, but any hope of rest and relaxation is ruined when Janey persuades them to let her have a party, prompting the put-upon parents to sneak back to the house and check up on her friends

programme.title: My Family
programme.sub-title: Death and Ben Take a Holiday
programme.desc: The death of Aunt Margaret leads the Harpers to arrange a weekend in Leeds to attend the funeral, in spite of the fact that Ben never liked her. However, thanks to reservation problems, Nick's shameless libido and a drug-addled Susan, events go from bad to worse

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Janey needs some driving experience as she swots up for her impending test. However, a disastrous road trip with Nick leaves her with no choice but to get into a car with dad Ben - and after the day he has just had, they are both certain to be driven round the bend

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: The Harpers are all set to redecorate, and even Janey's boyfriend Brian is asked to help. The next day, when his sleeping arrangements become apparent, it is not just the paint that starts flying

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Nick acquires a new girlfriend and the clan gets a bit of a shock when he brings her round for dinner - she's old enough to be his mother. Michael gets a new computer, while Janey starts to feel somewhat neglected, Comedy, starring Robert Lindsay, Zoe Wanamaker and Kris Marshall

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: The work of a pioneering veterinary practice, where 'Bionic Vet' Noel Fitzpatrick and a team of more than 100 surgeons, nurses, ward attendants and physiotherapists help some of the nation's hardest-to-cure pets through cutting-edge treatment and operations. This series opens with a special episode featuring the amazing stories - old and new - of some of the youngest and cutest puppies for whom Noel has provided life-saving treatment since The Supervet began in 2014, and the families at the centre of their inspiring stories

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: The series showcasing the extraordinary work of veterinary surgeon Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team at Fitzpatrick Referrals where families from all over the world seek hope and solutions for their animal friends. Two-year-old Boston terrier Rogan needs Noel's help with his debilitating hip dysplasia. Noel explains to Rogan's mums Megan and Shana that, due to Rogan's age and breed, an off-the-shelf cemented total hip replacement has an increased chance of loosening over time. Noel's offered alternative is a next-generation uncemented implant system, custom-made for Rogan. Handsome nine-year-old Hungarian vizsla Brodie is affected by developmental elbow disease. Noel finds that the joint has completely collapsed and is infected as the result of a previous botched operation by another vet. Poor Brodie is now in constant pain, but Noel is able to restore hope for Brodie and his family with a new customised implant system that will fuse the damaged bones together. And Vinnie the two-year-old tabby cat is rushed into the surgery after being hit by a car. Thankfully he has no internal injuries but he has sustained significant damage to his back legs. Noel and the team fight to repair the damage before infection can take hold

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Marbella-based seven-month-old expat French bulldog is flown in by private jet from Spain with his family after surgery to fix his broken elbow proved unsuccessful. Its family hopes a custom fusion plate system will get Milo out of pain. Eleven-year-old rescue brindle boxer Bramble is referred to Noel by a local vet after a mystery mass has been found eating away the bone around her knee. Owner Paula opts for an innovative total knee replacement, but Noel is then presented with a massive dilemma when the hound breaks her femur during recovery

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick steps out of his comfort zone by heading to South Africa to spend time with the magnificent animals of the savannah and the selfless people who dedicate their lives to looking after them - often in very difficult circumstances. Noel meets the animals threatened by poaching, injured due to neglect in captivity, and exploited in one way or another. The pioneering vet also helps endangered rhinos and improve the lives of two big cats by performing procedures rarely - if ever - provided for wild animals before

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Russian blue cat is brought into the surgery with a shattered jaw after a dog attack, and a man living in a homeless shelter brings in his best friend Spud, a Labrador cross with a lame back leg. Plus, Noel tries to help straighten a deformity in a six-month-old Chihuahua puppy's leg

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics are called to help a family involved in a car crash on a dark street and reassure a woman with dementia who's having a suspected heart attack

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Crews require support from the fire brigade to rescue a woman stuck inside her locked house. Plus, they help a child with a dangerously high temperature

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Lyn has an investigation of her womb after a recent biopsy revealed abnormal cells and Steve is worried about only getting a local anaesthetic before his varicose veins are removed

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Julia has a camera investigation of her womb to find out the reason for her heavy bleeding and Emma has a section of bone removed from her foot to cure a painful condition

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Comedy sketches, featuring Paul and Sam, the foul-mouthed Nan and Bernie the incompetent nurse

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Piers travels to Homestead Correctional Institution in Florida to meet Amber Wright, who, at the age of 15 conspired with a group of friends to murder her ex-boyfriend Seath Jackson, an innocent teenage boy who was beaten, shot, and knee-capped before his corpse was burnt and his remains thrown in a quarry. Later, Piers travels to meet Seath's parents in Summerfield, Florida, with their visceral anger standing in stark contrast to Amber's cool demeanour. It soon emerges that Amber was a central player in the plot to kill Seath, but will she take responsibility for her actions?

programme.title: Deadly Waters with Captain Lee
programme.desc: Three missing fingers point to the identity of a body found near Catalina Island, leading to a wealthy mogul, a luxurious yacht and an unlikely killer

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis interviews some of the biggest names in UK entertainment and is granted exclusive access into their lives at home and at work, beginning with platinum-selling rapper Stormzy. After two years out of the spotlight, Stormzy welcomes Louis into his home in south-west London to sit down for a frank and revealing conversation that covers many aspects of his life; from his childhood to the breakdown of a high-profile relationship, and the pressures that come with being one of the country's best-loved musicians and role models

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In Dudley, a man collapses while playing bingo two days before his wedding, while in Stoke, crews are called to help an elderly man who has had an unexplained fall.

programme.desc: The team are called in to save the survivors of a fatal crash and there's a bomb alert after Afghan asylum seekers are moved into a seaside hotel

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A car driven by teenage joyriders plunges into a deep ditch and a punter falls through the floor of his local betting shop in a bizarre accident

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: A boater jumps through a fireball after a gas explosion, a hunter has a heart attack, and fishermen get some much-needed advice

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: A mother and her unborn baby are at risk, adventure biking in the Marlborough Sounds causes a nasty injury, and a Land SAR team saves the day

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Fortune smiles on Mike and Alison when a TV company comes calling, looking for an old house in which to film a period drama. But to get the money in the bank, Alison must ensure the shoot goes without a hitch despite the horde of curious ghosts, the house's crumbling floorboards and her crippling crush on the show's lead actor. Meanwhile, Thomas and Lady Button learn the meaning of a free pass, and Julian tries his luck at betting on horse racing. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Learning that their posh neighbours the Beg-Chetwyndes have an ancient claim over their land, Mike and Alison throw an upmarket dinner party to help resolve a thorny issue. But with the ghosts her only guide to high-class decorum, the evening starts badly and only gets worse - thanks to Mary's woeful catering, Mike's clueless deal-making and Robin's raucous celebration of the lunar eclipse. Elsewhere, the spooks become obsessed with a TV sitcom. Comedy, guest starring Geoff McGivern and Sophie Thompson

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A luxury hotel chain makes penniless and exhausted couple Mike and Alison an offer they can't refuse, but the prospect of their departure drives a wedge between the ghosts, and the hardline Captain finds himself ostracised, while Pat is voted in as reluctant leader. As the clueless spirits brainstorm money-making schemes, the Captain takes matters into his own hands. Meanwhile, fortune smiles on Headless Humphrey in his ceaseless search for his errant body

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Comedy sketches, featuring Paul and Sam, the foul-mouthed Nan and Bernie the incompetent nurse

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show

programme.title: Deadly Waters with Captain Lee
programme.desc: Three missing fingers point to the identity of a body found near Catalina Island, leading to a wealthy mogul, a luxurious yacht and an unlikely killer

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: The Death Cleaners help the wife of an American football fan who has died but asked for help cleaning his 'man cave' before his passing

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Janine and Adam assist a woman who is threatening to jump out of a first-floor window, and husband and wife team Ann and Kevin are called out to help Ann's sick Dad

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: The arrival of French exchange student Sylvie makes for a decidedly unhappy family. Nick appoints himself a one-man welcoming committee and offers to put her up in his room, but Janey harbours ill feelings toward her, dashing hopes of improving Anglo-French relations

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben and Susan look forward to their well-deserved holiday, but any hope of rest and relaxation is ruined when Janey persuades them to let her have a party, prompting the put-upon parents to sneak back to the house and check up on her friends

programme.title: My Family
programme.sub-title: Death and Ben Take a Holiday
programme.desc: The death of Aunt Margaret leads the Harpers to arrange a weekend in Leeds to attend the funeral, in spite of the fact that Ben never liked her. However, thanks to reservation problems, Nick's shameless libido and a drug-addled Susan, events go from bad to worse

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Janey needs some driving experience as she swots up for her impending test. However, a disastrous road trip with Nick leaves her with no choice but to get into a car with dad Ben - and after the day he has just had, they are both certain to be driven round the bend

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: The Harpers are all set to redecorate, and even Janey's boyfriend Brian is asked to help. The next day, when his sleeping arrangements become apparent, it is not just the paint that starts flying

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Nick acquires a new girlfriend and the clan gets a bit of a shock when he brings her round for dinner - she's old enough to be his mother. Michael gets a new computer, while Janey starts to feel somewhat neglected, Comedy, starring Robert Lindsay, Zoe Wanamaker and Kris Marshall

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: The work of a pioneering veterinary practice, where 'Bionic Vet' Noel Fitzpatrick and a team of more than 100 surgeons, nurses, ward attendants and physiotherapists help some of the nation's hardest-to-cure pets through cutting-edge treatment and operations. This series opens with a special episode featuring the amazing stories - old and new - of some of the youngest and cutest puppies for whom Noel has provided life-saving treatment since The Supervet began in 2014, and the families at the centre of their inspiring stories

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: The series showcasing the extraordinary work of veterinary surgeon Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team at Fitzpatrick Referrals where families from all over the world seek hope and solutions for their animal friends. Two-year-old Boston terrier Rogan needs Noel's help with his debilitating hip dysplasia. Noel explains to Rogan's mums Megan and Shana that, due to Rogan's age and breed, an off-the-shelf cemented total hip replacement has an increased chance of loosening over time. Noel's offered alternative is a next-generation uncemented implant system, custom-made for Rogan. Handsome nine-year-old Hungarian vizsla Brodie is affected by developmental elbow disease. Noel finds that the joint has completely collapsed and is infected as the result of a previous botched operation by another vet. Poor Brodie is now in constant pain, but Noel is able to restore hope for Brodie and his family with a new customised implant system that will fuse the damaged bones together. And Vinnie the two-year-old tabby cat is rushed into the surgery after being hit by a car. Thankfully he has no internal injuries but he has sustained significant damage to his back legs. Noel and the team fight to repair the damage before infection can take hold

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Marbella-based seven-month-old expat French bulldog is flown in by private jet from Spain with his family after surgery to fix his broken elbow proved unsuccessful. Its family hopes a custom fusion plate system will get Milo out of pain. Eleven-year-old rescue brindle boxer Bramble is referred to Noel by a local vet after a mystery mass has been found eating away the bone around her knee. Owner Paula opts for an innovative total knee replacement, but Noel is then presented with a massive dilemma when the hound breaks her femur during recovery

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick steps out of his comfort zone by heading to South Africa to spend time with the magnificent animals of the savannah and the selfless people who dedicate their lives to looking after them - often in very difficult circumstances. Noel meets the animals threatened by poaching, injured due to neglect in captivity, and exploited in one way or another. The pioneering vet also helps endangered rhinos and improve the lives of two big cats by performing procedures rarely - if ever - provided for wild animals before

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Russian blue cat is brought into the surgery with a shattered jaw after a dog attack, and a man living in a homeless shelter brings in his best friend Spud, a Labrador cross with a lame back leg. Plus, Noel tries to help straighten a deformity in a six-month-old Chihuahua puppy's leg

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics are called to help a family involved in a car crash on a dark street and reassure a woman with dementia who's having a suspected heart attack

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Crews require support from the fire brigade to rescue a woman stuck inside her locked house. Plus, they help a child with a dangerously high temperature

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Lyn has an investigation of her womb after a recent biopsy revealed abnormal cells and Steve is worried about only getting a local anaesthetic before his varicose veins are removed

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Julia has a camera investigation of her womb to find out the reason for her heavy bleeding and Emma has a section of bone removed from her foot to cure a painful condition

programme.desc: A death row wife finds love with a convicted murderer, and new parents are determined not to let their baby run their lives. Peter Kay guest stars

programme.title: Who Killed Jenni Rivera?
programme.desc: In 2012, Latin music superstar Jenni Rivera was killed in a plane crash in Mexico - but was it an accident? Speculations about the crash continue today

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: The broadcaster meets Florida woman Jennifer Mee who, as a teenager, became a media sensation when she suffered a particularly strange ailment - a persistent case of hiccups. But in 2010, she lured a man to a dark alleyway where her friends were waiting to rob him. Instead he ended up being shot dead. Even though she didn't pull the trigger, and was nowhere near when the bullets were fired, Jennifer was sentenced to life without parole. Piers charts the journey through fame and rejection that saw Jennifer descend into a criminal lifestyle, and asks family members and legal experts if they believe her punishment was too harsh

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis interviews some of the biggest names in UK entertainment and is granted exclusive access into their lives at home and at work, beginning with platinum-selling rapper Stormzy. After two years out of the spotlight, Stormzy welcomes Louis into his home in south-west London to sit down for a frank and revealing conversation that covers many aspects of his life; from his childhood to the breakdown of a high-profile relationship, and the pressures that come with being one of the country's best-loved musicians and role models

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In Dudley, a man collapses while playing bingo two days before his wedding, while in Stoke, crews are called to help an elderly man who has had an unexplained fall.

programme.desc: Wolfie has to put his political opinions to one side as he prepares to meet his girlfriend's parents

programme.title: Indian Ocean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer reaches Sri Lanka, whose strategic location and tropical spices made it a target for invaders and colonisers for centuries. In the north he visits the scenes of vicious battles between the Tamil minority and the Sri Lankan army, traumatic events from which the population is still recovering. On his way to Bangladesh, he hitches a ride on a trawler, highlighting one of the Indian Ocean's fastest-growing industries - providing prawns for the West. But as he reveals, it comes at a price for the environment

programme.title: Indian Ocean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer begins the final leg of his journey on the northern tip of Sumatra in western Indonesia, near the epicentre of the 2004 Boxing Day tsunami, finding out how the province of Banda Aceh has undergone many changes since the disaster. He also explores the illegal trade in exotic pets in the Indonesian capital of Jakarta, before heading for Australia, the final country on his epic journey. There he visits the unspoilt wilderness of the Kimberley region, meets a crocodile hunter and goes fishing with natives campaigning to stop the construction of a giant gas plant

programme.title: Equator
programme.desc: Author and broadcaster Simon Reeve travels around the equator, beginning by visiting countries in Africa, including Gabon - a producer of oil that once had the world's highest per capita consumption of champagne. However, the lavish life comes to a halt when he and his team are abandoned by their drivers in the rainforest

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: This week tells the tale of the two Mercs, but what will the moral of the story be? Plus, the team get stuck into a moddable favourite - a 2006 Ford Fiesta ST

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek checks out an abandoned 1985 Renault 4 and an MGB Roadster that hasn't seen the light of day for 36 years, and Jack heads to Rotherham to pick up a pair of rare Bond Bugs.

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: A CBD80 battery-powered loco needs to be hauled 20 miles from a factory in Burton-on-Trent to the heritage Chasewater Railway in Staffordshire

programme.title: Take the Ball, Pass the Ball
programme.desc: An inside look at one of the most successful sides in club football history - Pep Guardiola's Barcelona - and the philosophy the led them to incredible triumphs

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Paul collects a cherished 1924 Rover 9 that's been in the same family since the 1950s and only had one previous owner before that

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads north to pick up a 1954 Morgan +4 coupe in mint condition and believed to be just one of three still on the road

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek waxes lyrical about a pristine Jaguar XJ8 and a Ford Model T van tests his driving skills

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Dom Chinea gives glass-blower Mark from north Wales and potter Victor from London the opportunity to turn their hobbies into money-making businesses. After being exposed to lead poisoning as a child Mark gave up his hobby of glass-blowing and spent 35 years working in the voluntary sector but when we was made redundant, Mark put his savings into setting up his own studio and getting back into glass-blowing. Victor was introduced to pottery by an art therapist during treatment for his anxiety. His clay sculptures have artistic flair but when he adds flourishes to his tableware it takes too long to make them profitable. The pair have two months to prove they have the skill and ambition to make it at market

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Dom Chinea helps artist Jack and potter Jeanet make their passions into money-making businesses. Jack is hoping to swap teaching for creating before it's too late, Dom and mentor Sharon are impressed with his multimedia artwork but are worried that he only seems to use his family as his subjects. University lecturer Jeanet struggles to find time to turn her hobby into a profession but mentor Florian thinks speed and finishing touches are what she needs to make a profit

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo crosses the south east of Australia from Canberra, the seat of government, to the multi-ethnic heart of its second largest city, Melbourne. Along the way he discovers the history of the Ashes at Melbourne cricket ground, learns about the exploits of outlaw Ned Kelly and visits the home of superstar soprano Dame Nellie Melba. He also rides on the Puffing Billy narrow gauge steam railway, built in 1900, and has a go at shearing a sheep

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels by rail through Queensland, marvelling at the engineering expertise of a railway line built in the 1890s. He visits a plantation where he learns how to harvest sugar cane, finds out how the townsfolk of Innisfail withstand the tropical storms that strike the region and ventures into the hinterland, where he braves a crocodile safari. At Mungalla Cattle Station, he hears how aboriginal people faced brutality at the hands of 19th-century settlers and after a singalong, he makes tracks for his end destination of Townsville - the gateway to the Great Barrier Reef

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels along the eastern coast of Australia from the coal mines of Newcastle, New South Wales, to Queensland's modern state capital, Brisbane. In the world's largest coal-exporting port, Michael marvels at the skill of the loader as he transfers thousands of tons of coal from rail to ship. He visits the Koala Hospital at Port Macquarie, where he finds populations of these marsupials are in rapid decline. Michael also visits a factory where the quintessential Australian bush hat is made and in Woolgoolga, he joins a Sikh community to hear how they have integrated into Australian society

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming begins a new canal adventure in Wales and encounters towering aqueducts and stunning scenery along the way

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming's journey on the Llangollen Canal takes an unexpected turn when his boat gets stuck in silt and a passing hire boat comes to the rescue

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming navigates the scenic Oxford Canal onboard Naughty Lass, explores its twists and turns, and helps tow a stranded broken-down boater

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: Two years after Cuba announced radical economic reforms, broadcaster Simon Reeve heads to the Caribbean island to look at the effects the liberalisation has had. He meets Cubans whose lives are being transformed - from the owners of fledgling businesses to the newly rich estate agents selling properties valued at up to £750,000 - and asks if this new openness could lead to more political freedom. He also explores whether Cuba will be able to maintain the positive aspects of its long isolation under socialism - such as low crime, high-quality education and one of the best healthcare systems in the world - while embracing a form of capitalism. Part of the This World strand

programme.title: Sacred Rivers with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer returns with a series that takes him around the world to areas that cannot be fully appreciated without considering the vast influence of their rivers, and he's going to explain how those sacred rivers don't just simply flow through countries - they define them. Simon begins at the source of the world's longest river, the Nile, and follows it out to sea, taking him from the Ethiopian highlands, through the desert of Sudan and then Egypt, right to the Mediterranean Sea. He also explains how the Nile has forged some of our earliest civilisations and influenced religions. However, the stability of the region looks uncertain, given that populations along its banks are growing, and Nile countries are demanding greater shares of the sacred waters

programme.title: Sacred Rivers with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer travels along the Ganges to explore how India's booming economy has affected its religious culture, starting his journey at the source of the river in the foothills of the Himalayas and following it to where it empties out into the Bay of Bengal. Along the way, he meets westerners in Rishikesh seeking spiritual enlightenment, takes a dip in the fast-flowing waters at Haridwar and discovers how the river is being severely polluted in Kanpur, where it is alleged that cows are secretly being slaughtered to fuel a booming leather trade

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie moors by Hampton Court Palace, but disaster strikes when a tree branch smashes a window. His dad joins to help through Hanwell locks

programme.title: Citizen Smith
programme.desc: Robert Lindsay stars in the first episode of John Sullivan's 1970s comedy about Wolfie Smith, a suburban revolutionary who dreams of becoming the next Che Guevara as leader of the Tooting Popular Front. Cheryl Hall, Peter Vaughan and Mike Grady co-star

programme.title: Citizen Smith
programme.desc: Wolfie has to put his political opinions to one side as he prepares to meet his girlfriend's parents

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming begins a new canal adventure in Wales and encounters towering aqueducts and stunning scenery along the way

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming navigates the scenic Oxford Canal onboard Naughty Lass, explores its twists and turns, and helps tow a stranded broken-down boater

programme.title: Indian Ocean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer reaches Sri Lanka, whose strategic location and tropical spices made it a target for invaders and colonisers for centuries. In the north he visits the scenes of vicious battles between the Tamil minority and the Sri Lankan army, traumatic events from which the population is still recovering. On his way to Bangladesh, he hitches a ride on a trawler, highlighting one of the Indian Ocean's fastest-growing industries - providing prawns for the West. But as he reveals, it comes at a price for the environment

programme.title: Indian Ocean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer begins the final leg of his journey on the northern tip of Sumatra in western Indonesia, near the epicentre of the 2004 Boxing Day tsunami, finding out how the province of Banda Aceh has undergone many changes since the disaster. He also explores the illegal trade in exotic pets in the Indonesian capital of Jakarta, before heading for Australia, the final country on his epic journey. There he visits the unspoilt wilderness of the Kimberley region, meets a crocodile hunter and goes fishing with natives campaigning to stop the construction of a giant gas plant

programme.title: Equator
programme.desc: Author and broadcaster Simon Reeve travels around the equator, beginning by visiting countries in Africa, including Gabon - a producer of oil that once had the world's highest per capita consumption of champagne. However, the lavish life comes to a halt when he and his team are abandoned by their drivers in the rainforest

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: This week tells the tale of the two Mercs, but what will the moral of the story be? Plus, the team get stuck into a moddable favourite - a 2006 Ford Fiesta ST

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek checks out an abandoned 1985 Renault 4 and an MGB Roadster that hasn't seen the light of day for 36 years, and Jack heads to Rotherham to pick up a pair of rare Bond Bugs.

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: A CBD80 battery-powered loco needs to be hauled 20 miles from a factory in Burton-on-Trent to the heritage Chasewater Railway in Staffordshire

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek brings a small army of military hardware to Thornton le Dale including a Studebaker Weasel tracked vehicle and a WWII-era Willys Jeep

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads north to pick up a 1954 Morgan +4 coupe in mint condition and believed to be just one of three still on the road

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek waxes lyrical about a pristine Jaguar XJ8 and a Ford Model T van tests his driving skills

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Dom Chinea gives glass-blower Mark from north Wales and potter Victor from London the opportunity to turn their hobbies into money-making businesses. After being exposed to lead poisoning as a child Mark gave up his hobby of glass-blowing and spent 35 years working in the voluntary sector but when we was made redundant, Mark put his savings into setting up his own studio and getting back into glass-blowing. Victor was introduced to pottery by an art therapist during treatment for his anxiety. His clay sculptures have artistic flair but when he adds flourishes to his tableware it takes too long to make them profitable. The pair have two months to prove they have the skill and ambition to make it at market

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Dom Chinea helps artist Jack and potter Jeanet make their passions into money-making businesses. Jack is hoping to swap teaching for creating before it's too late, Dom and mentor Sharon are impressed with his multimedia artwork but are worried that he only seems to use his family as his subjects. University lecturer Jeanet struggles to find time to turn her hobby into a profession but mentor Florian thinks speed and finishing touches are what she needs to make a profit

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo crosses the south east of Australia from Canberra, the seat of government, to the multi-ethnic heart of its second largest city, Melbourne. Along the way he discovers the history of the Ashes at Melbourne cricket ground, learns about the exploits of outlaw Ned Kelly and visits the home of superstar soprano Dame Nellie Melba. He also rides on the Puffing Billy narrow gauge steam railway, built in 1900, and has a go at shearing a sheep

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels by rail through Queensland, marvelling at the engineering expertise of a railway line built in the 1890s. He visits a plantation where he learns how to harvest sugar cane, finds out how the townsfolk of Innisfail withstand the tropical storms that strike the region and ventures into the hinterland, where he braves a crocodile safari. At Mungalla Cattle Station, he hears how aboriginal people faced brutality at the hands of 19th-century settlers and after a singalong, he makes tracks for his end destination of Townsville - the gateway to the Great Barrier Reef

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels along the eastern coast of Australia from the coal mines of Newcastle, New South Wales, to Queensland's modern state capital, Brisbane. In the world's largest coal-exporting port, Michael marvels at the skill of the loader as he transfers thousands of tons of coal from rail to ship. He visits the Koala Hospital at Port Macquarie, where he finds populations of these marsupials are in rapid decline. Michael also visits a factory where the quintessential Australian bush hat is made and in Woolgoolga, he joins a Sikh community to hear how they have integrated into Australian society

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming begins a new canal adventure in Wales and encounters towering aqueducts and stunning scenery along the way

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: Two years after Cuba announced radical economic reforms, broadcaster Simon Reeve heads to the Caribbean island to look at the effects the liberalisation has had. He meets Cubans whose lives are being transformed - from the owners of fledgling businesses to the newly rich estate agents selling properties valued at up to £750,000 - and asks if this new openness could lead to more political freedom. He also explores whether Cuba will be able to maintain the positive aspects of its long isolation under socialism - such as low crime, high-quality education and one of the best healthcare systems in the world - while embracing a form of capitalism. Part of the This World strand

programme.title: Sacred Rivers with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer returns with a series that takes him around the world to areas that cannot be fully appreciated without considering the vast influence of their rivers, and he's going to explain how those sacred rivers don't just simply flow through countries - they define them. Simon begins at the source of the world's longest river, the Nile, and follows it out to sea, taking him from the Ethiopian highlands, through the desert of Sudan and then Egypt, right to the Mediterranean Sea. He also explains how the Nile has forged some of our earliest civilisations and influenced religions. However, the stability of the region looks uncertain, given that populations along its banks are growing, and Nile countries are demanding greater shares of the sacred waters

programme.title: Sacred Rivers with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer travels along the Ganges to explore how India's booming economy has affected its religious culture, starting his journey at the source of the river in the foothills of the Himalayas and following it to where it empties out into the Bay of Bengal. Along the way, he meets westerners in Rishikesh seeking spiritual enlightenment, takes a dip in the fast-flowing waters at Haridwar and discovers how the river is being severely polluted in Kanpur, where it is alleged that cows are secretly being slaughtered to fuel a booming leather trade

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie moors by Hampton Court Palace, but disaster strikes when a tree branch smashes a window. His dad joins to help through Hanwell locks

programme.desc: Bob feels the strain of Thelma's absence, so Terry promptly suggests he move in until she decides to come home. Comedy, starring James Bolam, Rodney Bewes and Brigit Forsyth

programme.desc: Bob wallows in self-pity, convinced he will never be able to cope without Thelma and increasingly worried about what the neighbours will think of Terry's home-making efforts. Starring Rodney Bewes, James Bolam and Brigit Forsyth

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Hobbyist weaver has so far struggled to break into the textile market and mentor Piyush thinks he knows why. Jessica loves colour, but doesn't have a plan of how many she uses in her pieces. Piyush sets her a challenge of using no more than four colours in each of four seasonal collections. Nyanda is a passionate and talented jeweller with a strong design eye, but her business has stalled. Mentor Rachel wants her to make simpler pieces she can produce more quickly and sell for more affordable prices. Presented by Dominic Chinea

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Recently diagnosed with autism and dyslexia Shannon has only ever given the hand turned bowls she makes on her incredible foot-powered pole lathe as presents. Dom Chinea and mentor Alice love her work and the way she produces it and want to convince Shannon that she and the bowls she creates are much more marketable than she thinks. Successful musician Ryan wants to quit touring to spend more time at home with his young family and hopes an upcycling business will make that possible. Mentor Zoe can see his work is high quality, but is worried his style doesn't stand out from the crowd

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Three MGs from the 70s turn up at the garage, still covered in original factory wax. Nobody can believe it, or value them. A Morris 1000 turns up at the auction along with a TR7 V8

programme.title: Bangers & Cash

programme.title: Below Deck Sailing Yacht
programme.desc: Captain Glenn wants to improve the guest experience by insisting that a steward goes on all excursions, and Daisy is frustrated when she finds out that she's a steward down

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Mr Rose and Dr Ormerod attempt to rescue a drowning boy during a trip to a hippie commune. Catherine is asked on a date by a local fisherman, while Dr Goodwin discovers the parents of a sick baby are not all they seem after he diagnoses the infant's illness. Robert Daws, Denis Lill, Amelia Curtis and Paul Fox star

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Ruth Watson advises Keith and Val Morris on ways to revamp their failing hotel and conference centre the Hanmer Arms, which is losing money despite being large and well-equipped. She explains why the lack of a full-time receptionist and permanent head chef mean problems with continuity - and urges the reluctant and negative owners to boost their dwindling bookings by attracting corporate clients

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder follows the investigation into the suspicious death of 30-year-old Lyndsey Vaux, whose body was found in the Wigan home she shared with her partner Becky Reid and Becky's mum Gillian. Neighbours told police that the victim suffered regular abuse and beatings at the hands of her girlfriend. But when the pathology report proved inconclusive, police needed to find proof that she was responsible

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Joy's dream sends the three Merriwicks on a search for Phillip Harper, Joy's father. Fantasy drama, starring Catherine Bell

programme.title: Good Witch

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs

programme.title: What Would Your Kid Do?

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Most of the spaniels at Battersea Dogs and Cats Home are full of energy, but Paul meets one who was found tied up and has lost all hope. There's also a very special visitor to Battersea - comedian Harry Hill

programme.title: What Would Your Kid Do?
programme.desc: Jason Manford presents the show which asks how well people really know their kids, by challenging parents to guess what their children will do in a variety of entertaining situations. Each show sees youngsters filmed while taking part in a variety of classroom activities and games, unaware that we're actually testing everything from empathy and lateral thinking to curiosity and rule breaking

programme.desc: In Britain, around 11 million people - almost one in six of the population - are on some form of diet; many in a bid to slim down for a particular event. In this programme, Dr Xand van Tulleken and dietician Hala El-Shafie help six people go on the most hyped and written about diets on the market, with the volunteers divided into Crashers on short-term regimens, Shape Shifters on six-week programmes, and Life Changers on four-month plans. programme.title: Good Witch

Oscar-winner Jennifer Lawrence discusses her role in psychological drama Die My Love and Tessa Thompson reflects on taking the lead in Hedda, a bold re-imagining of Ibsen's Hedda Gabler. At the former she tries to help Rosa and Daniel, the new, but disengaged owners, while at the latter, she is faced with an establishment that has fallen behind the times and is stuck firmly in the past

programme.title: Who's Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Anthea Turner plays mystery host as she throws a dinner party for four strangers at her home. All they have to do is guess who has been cooking for them to win a cash prize and leave the TV presenter doing his own washing up. But if she manages to keep her identity intact, they'll be the ones pulling on the Marigolds. Presented by Brian McFadden

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The sports broadcaster talks to Tommy Martin about his childhood, his life as a footballer and his successful career as a journalist, radio host and TV pundit

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Magazine programme blending lifestyle interviews and news content with human interest stories, consumer affairs, health, politics, gardening, education, finance, food and fashion

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner get creative with the humble and versatile potato featuring in four exciting and inspiring recipes, starting with fish pie, and beans and cheese fillings for jacket potatoes. They then move on to a veggie cottage pie with lentils, a potato-based cheese and pancetta quiche, and cheeseburgers with potato buns that are both tempting and delicious

programme.title: Puppy Secrets: The First Six Months
programme.desc: Documentary charting the lives of four puppy litters in intimate detail, from birth to their sixth month. Owners and breeders join vet Mark Evans as he sheds light on what to expect from these young chihuahuas, sheep dogs, rescue puppies and assistance dogs as they grow into maturity. In the first of two programmes, cameras follow the pups during their first eight weeks of life, which include such milestones as opening their eyes for the first time, taking their first steps, and developing the crucial skill of 'bite inhibition'

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard asks the questions as Tony Blackburn, Tim Vine and Megan McKenna answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. programme.title: The Great Escapers

With the construction of a human tower, a couple of surprises from Denise, an aerial yoga lesson that isn't a zen-like experience for everyone and a forage for flowers in store, which of the Loose Ladies will win the title of Greatest Escaper when the three of them meet to decide the winner?

programme.title: Week in Review
programme.desc: A look back at the main local, national and international stories of the week

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts as a contestant tries to open the 22 red boxes in the right order, taking on the infamous Banker for the chance to win a life-changing cash prize

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the concluding live semi-final as more acts that made it through the auditions perform in front of the judges and an audience for a chance at a place in the final. programme.title: The Way
programme.desc: Drama set in the aftermath of a violent uprising in Port Talbot, and following a family forced to flee the ensuing violence. A shocking event spreads panic and a heated meeting prompts a strike. Fearing the unrest will spread into England, a lockdown is enforced on the Welsh border, causing further anger, ending in a protest march, and ultimately a violent battle on Station Road. Directed by and starring Michael Sheen

programme.title: Sweet Home Alabama (2002)
programme.desc: A successful New York fashion designer excitedly planning her wedding has to return to her home town in Alabama to persuade her first husband to sign the papers to finalise their divorce. However, seeing her childhood sweetheart stirs up old feelings, leaving the bride-to-be in a quandary over her future. Romantic comedy, starring Reese Witherspoon, Patrick Dempsey, Josh Lucas and Candice Bergen

programme.title: The Six O'Clock Show

programme.sub-title: Horse & Hound Country Inn and Tenison Towers Guest House
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi tackles two hotels at the same time - the Horse & Hound Country Inn in Bonchester Bridge, Hawick, and Tenison Towers Guest House in Cambridge. At the former she tries to help Rosa and Daniel, the new, but disengaged owners, while at the latter, she is faced with an establishment that has fallen behind the times and is stuck firmly in the past

programme.title: Who's Doing the Dishes?

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner get creative with the humble and versatile potato featuring in four exciting and inspiring recipes, starting with fish pie, and beans and cheese fillings for jacket potatoes. They then move on to a veggie cottage pie with lentils, a potato-based cheese and pancetta quiche, and cheeseburgers with potato buns that are both tempting and delicious

programme.title: Puppy Secrets: The First Six Months
programme.desc: Documentary charting the lives of four puppy litters in intimate detail, from birth to their sixth month. Owners and breeders join vet Mark Evans as he sheds light on what to expect from these young chihuahuas, sheep dogs, rescue puppies and assistance dogs as they grow into maturity. In the first of two programmes, cameras follow the pups during their first eight weeks of life, which include such milestones as opening their eyes for the first time, taking their first steps, and developing the crucial skill of 'bite inhibition'
